children.text: the weighted average number of full-time-equivalent residents (as determined under paragraph (4)) in the hospital’s approved medical residency training programs in that period.

children.heading: Medicare patient load
children.label: C Medicare patient load
children.text: As used in subparagraph (A), the term “medicare patient load” means, with respect to a hospital’s cost reporting period, the fraction of the total number of inpatient-bed-days (as established by the Secretary) during the period which are attributable to patients with respect to whom payment may be made under part A.

children.heading: Payment for managed care enrollees
children.label: D Payment for managed care enrollees
I
children.text: the aggregate approved amount (as defined in subparagraph (B)) for that period; and

children.label: II
children.text: the fraction of the total number of inpatient-bed days (as established by the Secretary) during the period which are attributable to such enrolled individuals.

children.label: I
children.text: 20 percent in 1998,

children.label: II
children.text: 40 percent in 1999,

children.label: III
children.text: 60 percent in 2000,

children.label: IV
children.text: 80 percent in 2001, and

children.label: V
children.text: 100 percent in 2002 and subsequent years.

children.heading: Proportional reduction for nursing and allied health education
children.label: iii Proportional reduction for nursing and allied health education
children.text: The Secretary shall estimate a proportional adjustment in payments to all hospitals determined under clauses (i) and (ii) for portions of cost reporting periods beginning in a year (beginning with 2000) such that the proportional adjustment reduces payments in an amount for such year equal to the total additional payment amounts for nursing and allied health education determined under subsection () for portions of cost reporting periods occurring in that year. In applying the preceding sentence for each of 2010 through 2019, the Secretary shall not take into account any increase in the total amount of such additional payment amounts for such nursing and allied health education for portions of cost reporting periods occurring in the year pursuant to the application of paragraph (2)(B)(ii) of such subsection.l

children.heading: Special rule for hospitals under reimbursement system
children.label: iv Special rule for hospitals under reimbursement system
children.text: The Secretary shall establish rules for the application of this subparagraph to a hospital reimbursed under a reimbursement system authorized under in the same manner as it would apply to the hospital if it were not reimbursed under such section.section 1395f(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Rules
children.label: A Rules
children.text: The Secretary shall establish rules consistent with this paragraph for the computation of the number of full-time-equivalent residents in an approved medical residency training program.

children.heading: Adjustment for part-year or part-time residents
children.label: B Adjustment for part-year or part-time residents
children.text: Such rules shall take into account individuals who serve as residents for only a portion of a period with a hospital or simultaneously with more than one hospital.

children.heading: Weighting factors for certain residents
children.label: C Weighting factors for certain residents
i
children.text: before , for each resident the weighting factor is 1.00,July 1, 1986

children.label: ii
children.text: on or after , for a resident who is in the resident’s initial residency period (as defined in paragraph (5)(F)), the weighting factor is 1.00,July 1, 1986

children.label: iii
children.text: on or after , and before , for a resident who is not in the resident’s initial residency period (as defined in paragraph (5)(F)), the weighting factor is .75, andJuly 1, 1986July 1, 1987

children.label: iv
children.text: on or after , for a resident who is not in the resident’s initial residency period (as defined in paragraph (5)(F)), the weighting factor is .50.July 1, 1987

children.heading: Foreign medical graduates required to pass FMGEMS examination
children.label: D Foreign medical graduates required to pass FMGEMS examination
I
children.text: the individual has passed the FMGEMS examination (as defined in paragraph (5)(E)), or

children.label: II
children.text: the individual has previously received certification from, or has previously passed the examination of, the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates.

children.label: I
children.text: has served as a resident before , and is serving as a resident after that date, butJuly 1, 1986

children.text: has not passed the FMGEMS examination or a previous examination of the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates before ,July 1, 1986

children.heading: Counting time spent in outpatient settings
children.label: E Counting time spent in outpatient settings
i
children.text: effective for cost reporting periods beginning before , all the time; so spent by a resident under an approved medical residency training program shall be counted towards the determination of full-time equivalency, without regard to the setting in which the activities are performed, if the hospital incurs all, or substantially all, of the costs for the training program in that setting; andJuly 1, 201010So in original. The semicolon probably should not appear.10

children.label: ii
children.text: effective for cost reporting periods beginning on or after , all the time so spent by a resident shall be counted towards the determination of full-time equivalency, without regard to the setting in which the activities are performed, if a hospital incurs the costs of the stipends and fringe benefits of the resident during the time the resident spends in that setting. If more than one hospital incurs these costs, either directly or through a third party, such hospitals shall count a proportional share of the time, as determined by written agreement between the hospitals, that a resident spends training in that setting.July 1, 2010

children.heading: Limitation on number of residents in allopathic and osteopathic medicine
children.label: F Limitation on number of residents in allopathic and osteopathic medicine

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Such rules shall provide that for purposes of a cost reporting period beginning on or after , subject to paragraphs (7), (8), (9), and (10), the total number of full-time equivalent residents before application of weighting factors (as determined under this paragraph) with respect to a hospital’s approved medical residency training program in the fields of allopathic medicine and osteopathic medicine may not exceed the number (or, 130 percent of such number in the case of a hospital located in a rural area) of such full-time equivalent residents for the hospital’s most recent cost reporting period ending on or before .October 1, 1997December 31, 1996

children.heading: Counting primary care residents on certain approved leaves of absence in base year FTE count
children.label: ii Counting primary care residents on certain approved leaves of absence in base year FTE count

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In determining the number of such full-time equivalent residents for a hospital’s most recent cost reporting period ending on or before , for purposes of clause (i), the Secretary shall count an individual to the extent that the individual would have been counted as a primary care resident for such period but for the fact that the individual, as determined by the Secretary, was on maternity or disability leave or a similar approved leave of absence.December 31, 1996

children.heading: Limitation to 3 FTE residents for any hospital
children.label: II Limitation to 3 FTE residents for any hospital
children.text: The total number of individuals counted under subclause (I) for a hospital may not exceed 3 full-time equivalent residents.

children.heading: Counting interns and residents for FY 1998 and subsequent years
children.label: G Counting interns and residents for FY 1998 and subsequent years

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For cost reporting periods beginning during fiscal years beginning on or after , subject to the limit described in subparagraph (F), the total number of full-time equivalent residents for determining a hospital’s graduate medical education payment shall equal the average of the actual full-time equivalent resident counts for the cost reporting period and the preceding two cost reporting periods.October 1, 1997

children.heading: Adjustment for short periods
children.label: ii Adjustment for short periods
children.text: If any cost reporting period beginning on or after , is not equal to twelve months, the Secretary shall make appropriate modifications to ensure that the average full-time equivalent resident counts pursuant to clause (i) are based on the equivalent of full twelve-month cost reporting periods.October 1, 1997

children.heading: Transition rule for 1998
children.label: iii Transition rule for 1998
children.text: In the case of a hospital’s first cost reporting period beginning on or after , clause (i) shall be applied by using the average for such period and the preceding cost reporting period.October 1, 1997

children.heading: Special rules for application of subparagraphs (F) and (G)
children.label: H Special rules for application of subparagraphs (F) and (G)
I
children.text: The Secretary shall, consistent with the principles of subparagraphs (F) and (G) and subject to paragraphs (7), (8), (9), and (10), prescribe rules for the application of such subparagraphs in the case of medical residency training programs established on or after . In promulgating such rules for purposes of subparagraph (F), the Secretary shall give special consideration to facilities that meet the needs of underserved rural areas.January 1, 1995

children.label: II
children.text: In applying this clause in the case of a hospital that, on or after , begins training residents in a new approved medical residency training program or programs (as defined by the Secretary), the Secretary shall not determine a limitation applicable to the hospital under subparagraph (F) until such time as the Secretary determines that the hospital has trained at least 1.0 full-time-equivalent resident in such new approved medical residency training program or programs in a cost reporting period.December 27, 2020

children.label: III
children.text: In applying this clause in the case of a hospital that, as of , has a limitation under subparagraph (F), based on a cost reporting period beginning before , of less than 1.0 full-time-equivalent resident, the Secretary shall adjust the limitation in the manner applicable to a new approved medical residency training program if the Secretary determines the hospital begins training at least 1.0 full-time-equivalent residents in a program year beginning on or after such date and before the date that is 5 years after such date.December 27, 2020October 1, 19979

children.label: IV
children.text: In applying this clause in the case of a hospital that, as of , has a limitation under subparagraph (F), based on a cost reporting period beginning on or after , and before such date, of no more than 3.0 full-time-equivalent residents, the Secretary shall adjust the limitation in the manner applicable to a new approved medical residency training program if the Secretary determines the hospital begins training more than 3.0 full-time-equivalent residents in a program year beginning on or after such date and before the date that is 5 years after such date.December 27, 2020October 1, 1997

children.label: V
children.text: An adjustment to the limitation applicable to a hospital made pursuant to subclause (III) or (IV) shall be made in a manner consistent with the methodology, as appropriate, in section 413.79(e) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation). As appropriate, the Secretary may consider information from any cost reporting periods necessary to make such an adjustment to the limitation.

children.heading: Aggregation
children.label: ii Aggregation
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe rules which allow institutions which are members of the same affiliated group (as defined by the Secretary) to elect to apply the limitation of subparagraph (F) on an aggregate basis.

children.heading: Data collection
children.label: iii Data collection
children.text: The Secretary may require any entity that operates a medical residency training program and to which subparagraphs (F) and (G) apply to submit to the Secretary such additional information as the Secretary considers necessary to carry out such subparagraphs.

children.heading: Training programs in rural areas
children.label: iv Training programs in rural areas

children.heading: Cost reporting periods beginning beforeOctober 1, 2022
children.label: I Cost reporting periods beginning beforeOctober 1, 2022
children.text: For cost reporting periods beginning before , in the case of a hospital that is not located in a rural area but establishes separately accredited approved medical residency training programs (or rural tracks) in a rural area or has an accredited training program with an integrated rural track, the Secretary shall adjust the limitation under subparagraph (F) in an appropriate manner insofar as it applies to such programs in such rural areas in order to encourage the training of physicians in rural areas.October 1, 2022

children.heading: Cost reporting periods beginning on or afterOctober 1, 2022
children.label: II Cost reporting periods beginning on or afterOctober 1, 2022
children.text: For cost reporting periods beginning on or after , in the case of a hospital not located in a rural area that established or establishes a medical residency training program (or rural tracks) in a rural area or establishes an accredited program where greater than 50 percent of the program occurs in a rural area, the Secretary shall consistent with the principles of subparagraphs (F) and (G) and subject to paragraphs (7) and (8), prescribe rules for the application of such subparagraphs with respect to such a program and, in accordance with such rules, adjust in an appropriate manner the limitation under subparagraph (F) for such hospital and each such hospital located in a rural area that participates in such a training.October 1, 202211So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.11

children.label: I
children.text: the limitation on the number of total full time equivalent residents under subparagraph (F) and clauses (v) and (vi)(I) of subsection (d)(5)(B) applicable to such provider shall be equal to the limitation applicable under such provisions to Provider No. 05–0578 for its cost reporting period ending on ; andJune 30, 2006

children.label: II
children.text: the provisions of subparagraph (G) and subsection (d)(5)(B)(vi)(II) shall not be applicable to such provider for the first three cost reporting years in which such provider trains residents under any approved medical residency training program.

children.heading: Redistribution of residency slots after a hospital closes
children.label: vi Redistribution of residency slots after a hospital closes

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding provisions of this clause, the Secretary shall, by regulation, establish a process under which, in the case where a hospital (other than a hospital described in clause (v)) with an approved medical residency program closes on or after a date that is 2 years before , the Secretary shall increase the otherwise applicable resident limit under this paragraph for other hospitals in accordance with this clause.March 23, 2010

children.heading: Priority for hospitals in certain areas
children.label: II Priority for hospitals in certain areas
aa
children.text: First, to hospitals located in the same core-based statistical area as, or a core-based statistical area contiguous to, the hospital that closed.

children.label: bb
children.text: Second, to hospitals located in the same State as the hospital that closed.

children.label: cc
children.text: Third, to hospitals located in the same region of the country as the hospital that closed.

children.label: dd
children.text: Fourth, only if the Secretary is not able to distribute the increase to hospitals described in item (cc), to qualifying hospitals in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (8).

children.heading: Requirement hospital likely to fill position within certain time period
children.label: III Requirement hospital likely to fill position within certain time period
children.text: The Secretary may only increase the otherwise applicable resident limit of a hospital under such process if the Secretary determines the hospital has demonstrated a likelihood of filling the positions made available under this clause within 3 years.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: IV Limitation
children.text: The aggregate number of increases in the otherwise applicable resident limits for hospitals under this clause shall be equal to the number of resident positions in the approved medical residency programs that closed on or after the date described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Administration
children.label: V Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the implementation of this clause.

children.heading: Treatment of certain nonprovider and didactic activities12So in original. No subpar. (I) has been enacted.12
children.label: J Treatment of certain nonprovider and didactic activities12So in original. No subpar. (I) has been enacted.12
children.text: Such rules shall provide that all time spent by an intern or resident in an approved medical residency training program in a nonprovider setting that is primarily engaged in furnishing patient care (as defined in paragraph (5)(K)) in non-patient care activities, such as didactic conferences and seminars, but not including research not associated with the treatment or diagnosis of a particular patient, as such time and activities are defined by the Secretary, shall be counted toward the determination of full-time equivalency.

children.heading: Treatment of certain other activities
children.label: K Treatment of certain other activities
children.text: In determining the hospital’s number of full-time equivalent residents for purposes of this subsection, all the time that is spent by an intern or resident in an approved medical residency training program on vacation, sick leave, or other approved leave, as such time is defined by the Secretary, and that does not prolong the total time the resident is participating in the approved program beyond the normal duration of the program shall be counted toward the determination of full-time equivalency.

children.heading: Approved medical residency training program
children.label: A Approved medical residency training program
children.text: The term “approved medical residency training program” means a residency or other postgraduate medical training program participation in which may be counted toward certification in a specialty or subspecialty and includes formal postgraduate training programs in geriatric medicine approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Consumer price index
children.label: B Consumer price index
children.text: The term “consumer price index” refers to the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (United States city average), as published by the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: Direct graduate medical education costs
children.label: C Direct graduate medical education costs
children.text: The term “direct graduate medical education costs” means direct costs of approved educational activities for approved medical residency training programs.

children.label: i
children.text: a school of medicine accredited by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education of the American Medical Association and the Association of American Medical Colleges (or approved by such Committee as meeting the standards necessary for such accreditation),

children.label: ii
children.text: a school of osteopathy accredited by the American Osteopathic Association, or approved by such Association as meeting the standards necessary for such accreditation, or

children.label: iii
children.text: a school of dentistry or podiatry which is accredited (or meets the standards for accreditation) by an organization recognized by the Secretary for such purpose.

children.heading: FMGEMS examination
children.label: E FMGEMS examination
children.text: The term “FMGEMS examination” means parts I and II of the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination in the Medical Sciences or any successor examination recognized by the Secretary for this purpose.

children.label: i
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), in no case shall the initial period of residency exceed an aggregate period of formal training of more than five years for any individual, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a period, of not more than two years, during which an individual is in a geriatric residency or fellowship program or a preventive medicine residency or fellowship program which meets such criteria as the Secretary may establish, shall be treated as part of the initial residency period, but shall not be counted against any limitation on the initial residency period.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: i General rule
children.text: Subject to clauses (ii), (iii), (iv), and (v), the term “period of board eligibility” means, for a resident, the minimum number of years of formal training necessary to satisfy the requirements for initial board eligibility in the particular specialty for which the resident is training.

children.heading: Application of 1985–1986 directory
children.label: ii Application of 1985–1986 directory
children.text: Except as provided in clause (iii), the period of board eligibility shall be such period specified in the 1985–1986 Directory of Residency Training Programs published by the Accreditation Council on Graduate Medical Education.

children.heading: Changes in period of board eligibility
children.label: iii Changes in period of board eligibility
I
children.text: increases the minimum number of years of formal training necessary to satisfy the requirements for a specialty, above the period specified in its 1985–1986 Directory, the Secretary may increase the period of board eligibility for that specialty, but not to exceed the period of board eligibility specified in that later Directory, or

children.label: II
children.text: decreases the minimum number of years of formal training necessary to satisfy the requirements for a specialty, below the period specified in its 1985–1986 Directory, the Secretary may decrease the period of board eligibility for that specialty, but not below the period of board eligibility specified in that later Directory.

children.heading: Special rule for certain primary care combined residency programs
children.label: iv Special rule for certain primary care combined residency programs
I
children.text: In the case of a resident enrolled in a combined medical residency training program in which all of the individual programs (that are combined) are for training a primary care resident (as defined in subparagraph (H)), the period of board eligibility shall be the minimum number of years of formal training required to satisfy the requirements for initial board eligibility in the longest of the individual programs plus one additional year.

children.label: II
children.text: A resident enrolled in a combined medical residency training program that includes an obstetrics and gynecology program shall qualify for the period of board eligibility under subclause (I) if the other programs such resident combines with such obstetrics and gynecology program are for training a primary care resident.

children.heading: Child neurology training programs
children.label: v Child neurology training programs
children.text: In the case of a resident enrolled in a child neurology residency training program, the period of board eligibility and the initial residency period shall be the period of board eligibility for pediatrics plus 2 years.

children.heading: Primary care resident
children.label: H Primary care resident
children.text: The term “primary care resident” means a resident enrolled in an approved medical residency training program in family medicine, general internal medicine, general pediatrics, preventive medicine, geriatric medicine, or osteopathic general practice.

children.heading: Resident
children.label: I Resident
children.text: The term “resident” includes an intern or other participant in an approved medical residency training program.

children.heading: Adjustments for certain family practice residency programs
children.label: J Adjustments for certain family practice residency programs
I
children.text: provide for an average amount under paragraph (2)(A) that takes into account the Secretary’s estimate of the amount that would have been recognized as reasonable under this subchapter if the hospital had not received such funds, and

children.label: II
children.text: reduce the payment amount otherwise provided under this subsection in an amount equal to the proportion of such program funds received during the cost reporting period involved that is allocable to this subchapter.

children.label: I
children.text: the program is limited to training for family and community medicine;

children.label: II
children.text: the program is the only approved medical residency program of the hospital; and

children.label: III
children.text: the average amount determined under paragraph (2)(A) for the hospital (as determined without regard to the increase in such amount described in clause (i)(I)) does not exceed $10,000.

children.heading: Nonprovider setting that is primarily engaged in furnishing patient care
children.label: K Nonprovider setting that is primarily engaged in furnishing patient care
children.text: The term “nonprovider setting that is primarily engaged in furnishing patient care” means a nonprovider setting in which the primary activity is the care and treatment of patients, as defined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Incentive payment under plans for voluntary reduction in number of residents
children.label: 6 Incentive payment under plans for voluntary reduction in number of residents
I
children.text: the amount of payment which would have been made under this subsection if there had been a 5-percent reduction in the number of full-time equivalent residents in the approved medical education training programs of the hospital as of , exceedsJune 30, 1997

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of payment which is made under this subsection, taking into account the reduction in such number effected under the reduction plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the reduction in payment under subsection (d)(5)(B) for the hospital that is attributable to the reduction in number of residents effected under the plan below 95 percent of the number of full-time equivalent residents in such programs of the hospital as of .June 30, 1997

children.label: i
children.text: the application is submitted in a form and manner specified by the Secretary and by not later than ,November 1, 199913So in original. The comma probably should be a semicolon.13

children.label: ii
children.text: the application provides for the operation of a plan for the reduction in the number of full-time equivalent residents in the approved medical residency training programs of the entity consistent with the requirements of subparagraph (D);

children.label: iii
children.text: the entity elects in the application the period of residency training years (not greater than 5) over which the reduction will occur;

children.label: iv
children.text: the entity will not reduce the proportion of its residents in primary care (to the total number of residents) below such proportion as in effect as of the applicable time described in subparagraph (D)(v); and

children.label: v
children.text: the Secretary determines that the application and the entity and such plan meet such other requirements as the Secretary specifies in regulations.

children.label: i
children.text: Individual hospitals operating one or more approved medical residency training programs.

children.label: ii
children.text: Two or more hospitals that operate such programs and apply for treatment under this paragraph as a single qualifying entity.

children.label: iii
children.text: A qualifying consortium (as described in section 4628 of the Balanced Budget Act of 1997).

children.label: I
children.text: If the base number of residents exceeds 750 residents, by a number equal to at least 20 percent of such base number.

children.label: II
children.text: Subject to subclause (IV), if the base number of residents exceeds 600 but is less than 750 residents, by 150 residents.

children.label: III
children.text: Subject to subclause (IV), if the base number of residents does not exceed 600 residents, by a number equal to at least 25 percent of such base number.

children.label: IV
children.text: In the case of a qualifying entity which is described in clause (v) and which elects treatment under this subclause, by a number equal to at least 20 percent of the base number.

children.label: I
children.text: Subject to subclause (II), by a number equal to at least 25 percent of the base number.

children.label: II
children.text: In the case of such a qualifying entity which is described in clause (v) and which elects treatment under this subclause, by a number equal to at least 20 percent of the base number.

children.heading: Consortia
children.label: iii Consortia
children.text: In the case of a qualifying entity described in subparagraph (C)(iii), the number of full-time equivalent residents in the aggregate for all the approved medical residency training programs operated by or through the entity shall be reduced by a number equal to at least 20 percent of the base number.

children.heading: Manner of reduction
children.label: iv Manner of reduction
children.text: The reductions specified under the preceding provisions of this subparagraph for a qualifying entity shall be below the base number of residents for that entity and shall be fully effective not later than the 5th residency training year in which the application under subparagraph (B) is effective.

children.heading: Entities providing assurance of increase in primary care residents
children.label: v Entities providing assurance of increase in primary care residents
I
children.text: the base number of residents for the entity is less than 750 or the entity is described in subparagraph (C)(ii); and

children.label: II
children.text: the entity represents in its application under subparagraph (B) that it will increase the number of full-time equivalent residents in primary care by at least 20 percent (from such number included in the base number of residents) by not later than the 5th residency training year in which the application under subparagraph (B) is effective.

children.heading: “Base number of residents” defined
children.label: vi “Base number of residents” defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “base number of residents” means, with respect to a qualifying entity (or its participating hospitals) operating approved medical residency training programs, the number of full-time equivalent residents in such programs (before application of weighting factors) of the entity as of the most recent residency training year ending before , or, if less, for any subsequent residency training year that ends before the date the entity makes application under this paragraph.June 30, 1997

children.label: i
children.text: first and second residency training years in which the reduction plan is in effect, 100 percent,

children.label: ii
children.text: third such year, 75 percent,

children.label: iii
children.text: fourth such year, 50 percent, and

children.label: iv
children.text: fifth such year, 25 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No payment may be made under this paragraph to a hospital for a residency training year if the hospital has failed to reduce the number of full-time equivalent residents (in the manner required under subparagraph (D)) to the number agreed to by the Secretary and the qualifying entity in approving the application under this paragraph with respect to such year.

children.heading: Increase in number of residents in subsequent years
children.label: ii Increase in number of residents in subsequent years
children.text: If payments are made under this paragraph to a hospital, and if the hospital increases the number of full-time equivalent residents above the number of such residents permitted under the reduction plan as of the completion of the plan, then, as specified by the Secretary, the entity is liable for repayment to the Secretary of the total amounts paid under this paragraph to the entity.

children.heading: Treatment of rotating residents
children.label: G Treatment of rotating residents
children.text: In applying this paragraph, the Secretary shall establish rules regarding the counting of residents who are assigned to institutions the medical residency training programs in which are not covered under approved applications under this paragraph.

children.heading: Reduction in limit based on unused positions
children.label: A Reduction in limit based on unused positions

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II), if a hospital’s reference resident level (specified in clause (ii)) is less than the otherwise applicable resident limit (as defined in subparagraph (C)(ii)), effective for portions of cost reporting periods occurring on or after , the otherwise applicable resident limit shall be reduced by 75 percent of the difference between such otherwise applicable resident limit and such reference resident level.July 1, 2005

children.heading: Exception for small rural hospitals
children.label: II Exception for small rural hospitals
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to a hospital located in a rural area (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)(ii)) with fewer than 250 acute care inpatient beds.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in subclauses (II) and (III), the reference resident level specified in this clause for a hospital is the resident level for the most recent cost reporting period of the hospital ending on or before , for which a cost report has been settled (or, if not, submitted (subject to audit)), as determined by the Secretary.September 30, 2002

children.heading: Use of most recent accounting period to recognize expansion of existing programs
children.label: II Use of most recent accounting period to recognize expansion of existing programs
children.text: If a hospital submits a timely request to increase its resident level due to an expansion of an existing residency training program that is not reflected on the most recent settled cost report, after audit and subject to the discretion of the Secretary, the reference resident level for such hospital is the resident level for the cost reporting period that includes , as determined by the Secretary.July 1, 2003

children.heading: Expansions under newly approved programs
children.label: III Expansions under newly approved programs
children.text: Upon the timely request of a hospital, the Secretary shall adjust the reference resident level specified under subclause (I) or (II) to include the number of medical residents that were approved in an application for a medical residency training program that was approved by an appropriate accrediting organization (as determined by the Secretary) before , but which was not in operation during the cost reporting period used under subclause (I) or (II), as the case may be, as determined by the Secretary.January 1, 2002

children.heading: Affiliation
children.label: iii Affiliation
children.text: The provisions of clause (i) shall be applied to hospitals which are members of the same affiliated group (as defined by the Secretary under paragraph (4)(H)(ii)) as of .July 1, 2003

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to increase the otherwise applicable resident limit for each qualifying hospital that submits a timely application under this subparagraph by such number as the Secretary may approve for portions of cost reporting periods occurring on or after . The aggregate number of increases in the otherwise applicable resident limits under this subparagraph may not exceed the Secretary’s estimate of the aggregate reduction in such limits attributable to subparagraph (A).July 1, 2005

children.heading: Considerations in redistribution
children.label: ii Considerations in redistribution
children.text: In determining for which hospitals the increase in the otherwise applicable resident limit is provided under clause (i), the Secretary shall take into account the demonstrated likelihood of the hospital filling the positions within the first 3 cost reporting periods beginning on or after , made available under this subparagraph, as determined by the Secretary.July 1, 2005

children.heading: Priority for rural and small urban areas
children.label: iii Priority for rural and small urban areas
I
children.text: First, to hospitals located in rural areas (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)(ii)).

children.label: II
children.text: Second, to hospitals located in urban areas that are not large urban areas (as defined for purposes of subsection (d)).

children.label: III
children.text: Third, to other hospitals in a State if the residency training program involved is in a specialty for which there are not other residency training programs in the State.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iv Limitation
children.text: In no case shall more than 25 full-time equivalent additional residency positions be made available under this subparagraph with respect to any hospital.

children.heading: Application of locality adjusted national average per resident amount
children.label: v Application of locality adjusted national average per resident amount
children.text: With respect to additional residency positions in a hospital attributable to the increase provided under this subparagraph, notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, the approved FTE resident amount is deemed to be equal to the locality adjusted national average per resident amount computed under paragraph (4)(E) for that hospital.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: vi Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed as permitting the redistribution of reductions in residency positions attributable to voluntary reduction programs under paragraph (6), under a demonstration project approved as of , under the authority of , or as affecting the ability of a hospital to establish new medical residency training programs under paragraph (4)(H).October 31, 2003section 402 of Public Law 90–248

children.heading: Resident level and limit defined
children.label: C Resident level and limit defined

children.heading: Resident level
children.label: i Resident level
children.text: The term “resident level” means, with respect to a hospital, the total number of full-time equivalent residents, before the application of weighting factors (as determined under paragraph (4)), in the fields of allopathic and osteopathic medicine for the hospital.

children.heading: Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.label: ii Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.text: The term “otherwise applicable resident limit” means, with respect to a hospital, the limit otherwise applicable under subparagraphs (F)(i) and (H) of paragraph (4) on the resident level for the hospital determined without regard to this paragraph.

children.heading: Adjustment based on settled cost report
children.label: D Adjustment based on settled cost report
i
children.text: the otherwise applicable resident limit was reduced under subparagraph (A)(i)(I); and

children.label: ii
children.text: such reduction was based on a reference resident level that was determined using a cost report and where a revised or corrected notice of program reimbursement was issued for such cost report between and , whether as a result of an appeal or otherwise, and the reference resident level under such settled cost report is higher than the level used for the reduction under subparagraph (A)(i)(I);September 1, 2006September 15, 2006

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: E Judicial review
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under , 1395 of this title, or otherwise, with respect to determinations made under this paragraph, paragraph (8), paragraph (10), clause (i), (ii), (iii), or (v) of paragraph (2)(F), or clause (i) or (vi) of paragraph (4)(H).section 1395ff of this title14ooSo in original. Probably should be followed by “paragraph (9),”. See 2020 Amendment notes below.14

children.heading: Reductions in limit based on unused positions
children.label: A Reductions in limit based on unused positions

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), if a hospital’s reference resident level (as defined in subparagraph (H)(i)) is less than the otherwise applicable resident limit (as defined in subparagraph (H)(iii)), effective for portions of cost reporting periods occurring on or after , the otherwise applicable resident limit shall be reduced by 65 percent of the difference between such otherwise applicable resident limit and such reference resident level.July 1, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: a hospital located in a rural area (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)(ii)) with fewer than 250 acute care inpatient beds;

children.label: II
children.text: a hospital that was part of a qualifying entity which had a voluntary residency reduction plan approved under paragraph (6)(B) or under the authority of , if the hospital demonstrates to the Secretary that it has a specified plan in place for filling the unused positions by not later than 2 years after ; orsection 402 of Public Law 90–248March 23, 2010

children.label: III
children.text: a hospital described in paragraph (4)(H)(v).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall increase the otherwise applicable resident limit for each qualifying hospital that submits an application under this subparagraph by such number as the Secretary may approve for portions of cost reporting periods occurring on or after . The aggregate number of increases in the otherwise applicable resident limit under this subparagraph shall be equal to the aggregate reduction in such limits attributable to subparagraph (A) (as estimated by the Secretary).July 1, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: the number of full-time equivalent primary care residents, as defined in paragraph (5)(H) (as determined by the Secretary), excluding any additional positions under subclause (II), is not less than the average number of full-time equivalent primary care residents (as so determined) during the 3 most recent cost reporting periods ending prior to ; andMarch 23, 2010

children.label: II
children.text: not less than 75 percent of the positions attributable to such increase are in a primary care or general surgery residency (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: Redistribution of positions if hospital no longer meets certain requirements
children.label: iii Redistribution of positions if hospital no longer meets certain requirements
I
children.text: reduce the otherwise applicable resident limit of the hospital by the amount by which such limit was increased under this paragraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: provide for the distribution of positions attributable to such reduction in accordance with the requirements of this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: the demonstration likelihood of the hospital filling the positions made available under this paragraph within the first 3 cost reporting periods beginning on or after , as determined by the Secretary; andJuly 1, 2011

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the hospital has an accredited rural training track (as described in paragraph (4)(H)(iv)).

children.label: i
children.text: Whether the hospital is located in a State with a resident-to-population ratio in the lowest quartile (as determined by the Secretary).

children.label: I
children.text: the total population of the State, territory, or District living in an area designated (under such section 332(a)(1)(A) ) as a health professional shortage area (as of ); to4March 23, 2010

children.label: II
children.text: the total population of the State, territory, or District (as determined by the Secretary based on the most recent available population data published by the Bureau of the Census).

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the hospital is located in a rural area (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)(ii)).

children.heading: Reservation of positions for certain hospitals
children.label: E Reservation of positions for certain hospitals
I
children.text: 70 percent of such positions for distribution to hospitals described in clause (i) of subparagraph (D).

children.label: II
children.text: 30 percent of such positions for distribution to hospitals described in clause (ii) and (iii) of such subparagraph.

children.heading: Exception if positions not redistributed byJuly 1, 2011
children.label: ii Exception if positions not redistributed byJuly 1, 2011
children.text: In the case where the Secretary does not distribute positions to hospitals in accordance with clause (i) by , the Secretary shall distribute such positions to other hospitals in accordance with the considerations described in subparagraph (C) and the priority described in subparagraph (D).July 1, 2011

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: F Limitation
children.text: A hospital may not receive more than 75 full-time equivalent additional residency positions under this paragraph.

children.heading: Application of per resident amounts for primary care and nonprimary care
children.label: G Application of per resident amounts for primary care and nonprimary care
children.text: With respect to additional residency positions in a hospital attributable to the increase provided under this paragraph, the approved FTE per resident amounts are deemed to be equal to the hospital per resident amounts for primary care and nonprimary care computed under paragraph (2)(D) for that hospital.

children.heading: Reference resident level
children.label: i Reference resident level
children.text: The term “reference resident level” means, with respect to a hospital, the highest resident level for any of the 3 most recent cost reporting periods (ending before ) of the hospital for which a cost report has been settled (or, if not, submitted (subject to audit)), as determined by the Secretary.March 23, 2010

children.heading: Resident level
children.label: ii Resident level
children.text: The term “resident level” has the meaning given such term in paragraph (7)(C)(i).

children.heading: Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.label: iii Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.text: The term “otherwise applicable resident limit” means, with respect to a hospital, the limit otherwise applicable under subparagraphs (F)(i) and (H) of paragraph (4) on the resident level for the hospital determined without regard to this paragraph but taking into account paragraph (7)(A).

children.heading: Affiliation
children.label: I Affiliation
children.text: The provisions of this paragraph shall be applied to hospitals which are members of the same affiliated group (as defined by the Secretary under paragraph (4)(H)(ii)) and the reference resident level for each such hospital shall be the reference resident level with respect to the cost reporting period that results in the smallest difference between the reference resident level and the otherwise applicable resident limit.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For fiscal year 2023, and for each succeeding fiscal year until the aggregate number of full-time equivalent residency positions distributed under this paragraph is equal to the aggregate number of such positions made available (as specified in clause (ii)(I)), the Secretary shall, subject to the succeeding provisions of this paragraph, increase the otherwise applicable resident limit for each qualifying hospital (as defined in subparagraph (F)) that submits a timely application under this subparagraph by such number as the Secretary may approve effective beginning July 1 of the fiscal year of the increase.

children.heading: Total number available
children.label: I Total number available
children.text: The aggregate number of such positions made available under this paragraph shall be equal to 1,000.

children.heading: Annual limit
children.label: II Annual limit
children.text: The aggregate number of such positions so made available shall not exceed 200 for a fiscal year.

children.heading: Rounds of applications
children.label: I Rounds of applications
children.text: The Secretary shall initiate a separate round of applications for an increase under clause (i) for each fiscal year for which such an increase is to be provided.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: II Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall notify hospitals of the number of positions distributed to the hospital under this paragraph as a result of an increase in the otherwise applicable resident limit by January 31 of the fiscal year of the increase. Such increase shall be effective beginning July 1 of such fiscal year.

children.heading: Considerations in distribution
children.label: i Considerations in distribution
children.text: In determining for which qualifying hospitals such an increase is provided under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall take into account the demonstrated likelihood of the hospital filling the positions made available under this paragraph within the first 5 training years beginning after the date the increase would be effective, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Minimum distribution for certain categories of hospitals
children.label: ii Minimum distribution for certain categories of hospitals
I
children.text: Hospitals that are located in a rural area (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)) or are treated as being located in a rural area pursuant to subsection (d)(8)(E).

children.label: II
children.text: Hospitals in which the reference resident level of the hospital (as specified in subparagraph (F)(iii)) is greater than the otherwise applicable resident limit.

children.label: aa
children.text: new medical schools that received “Candidate School” status from the Liaison Committee on Medical Education or that received “Pre-Accreditation” status from the American Osteopathic Association Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation on or after , and that have achieved or continue to progress toward “Full Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education) or toward “Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the American Osteopathic Association Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation); orJanuary 1, 2000

children.label: bb
children.text: additional locations and branch campuses established on or after , by medical schools with “Full Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education) or “Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the American Osteopathic Association Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation).January 1, 2000

children.label: IV
children.text: Hospitals that serve areas designated as health professional shortage areas under , as determined by the Secretary.section 254e(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A hospital may not receive more than 25 additional full-time equivalent residency positions under this paragraph.

children.heading: Prohibition on distribution to hospitals without an increase agreement
children.label: ii Prohibition on distribution to hospitals without an increase agreement
children.text: No increase in the otherwise applicable resident limit of a hospital may be made under this paragraph unless such hospital agrees to increase the total number of full-time equivalent residency positions under the approved medical residency training program of such hospital by the number of such positions made available by such increase under this paragraph.

children.heading: Application of per resident amounts for primary care and nonprimary care
children.label: D Application of per resident amounts for primary care and nonprimary care
children.text: With respect to additional residency positions in a hospital attributable to the increase provided under this paragraph, the approved FTE per resident amounts are deemed to be equal to the hospital per resident amounts for primary care and nonprimary care computed under paragraph (2)(D) for that hospital.

children.heading: Permitting facilities to apply aggregation rules
children.label: E Permitting facilities to apply aggregation rules
children.text: The Secretary shall permit hospitals receiving additional residency positions attributable to the increase provided under this paragraph to, beginning in the fifth year after the effective date of such increase, apply such positions to the limitation amount under paragraph (4)(F) that may be aggregated pursuant to paragraph (4)(H) among members of the same affiliated group.

children.heading: Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.label: i Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.text: The term “otherwise applicable resident limit” means, with respect to a hospital, the limit otherwise applicable under subparagraphs (F)(i) and (H) of paragraph (4) on the resident level for the hospital determined without regard to this paragraph but taking into account paragraphs (7)(A), (7)(B), (8)(A), and (8)(B).

children.heading: Qualifying hospital
children.label: ii Qualifying hospital
children.text: The term “qualifying hospital” means a hospital described in any of subclauses (I) through (IV) of subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: Reference resident level
children.label: iii Reference resident level
children.text: The term “reference resident level” means, with respect to a hospital, the resident level for the most recent cost reporting period of the hospital ending on or before , for which a cost report has been settled (or, if not, submitted (subject to audit)), as determined by the Secretary.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Resident level
children.label: iv Resident level
children.text: The term “resident level” has the meaning given such term in paragraph (7)(C)(i).

children.heading: Distribution of additional residency positions in psychiatry and psychiatry subspecialties
children.label: 10 Distribution of additional residency positions in psychiatry and psychiatry subspecialties

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For fiscal year 2026, the Secretary shall, subject to the succeeding provisions of this paragraph, increase the otherwise applicable resident limit for each qualifying hospital (as defined in subparagraph (F)) that submits a timely application under this subparagraph by such number as the Secretary may approve effective beginning July 1 of the fiscal year of the increase.

children.heading: Number available for distribution
children.label: ii Number available for distribution
children.text: The aggregate number of such positions made available under this paragraph shall be equal to 200.

children.heading: Distribution for psychiatry or psychiatry subspecialty residencies
children.label: iii Distribution for psychiatry or psychiatry subspecialty residencies
children.text: At least 100 of the positions made available under this paragraph shall be distributed for a psychiatry or psychiatry subspecialty residency (as defined in subparagraph (F)).

children.heading: Timing
children.label: iv Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall notify hospitals of the number of positions distributed to the hospital under this paragraph as a result of an increase in the otherwise applicable resident limit by January 31 of the fiscal year of the increase. Such increase shall be effective beginning July 1 of such fiscal year.

children.heading: Considerations in distribution
children.label: i Considerations in distribution
children.text: In determining for which qualifying hospitals such an increase is provided under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall take into account the demonstrated likelihood of the hospital filling the positions made available under this paragraph within the first 5 training years beginning after the date the increase would be effective, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Minimum distribution for certain categories of hospitals
children.label: ii Minimum distribution for certain categories of hospitals
I
children.text: Hospitals that are located in a rural area (as defined in ) or are treated as being located in a rural area pursuant to .section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this titlesection 1395ww(d)(8)(E) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: Hospitals in which the reference resident level of the hospital (as specified in subparagraph (F)(iii)) is greater than the otherwise applicable resident limit.

children.label: aa
children.text: new medical schools that received “Candidate School” status from the Liaison Committee on Medical Education or that received “Pre-Accreditation” status from the American Osteopathic Association Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation on or after , and that have achieved or continue to progress toward “Full Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education) or toward “Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the American Osteopathic Association Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation); orJanuary 1, 2000

children.label: bb
children.text: additional locations and branch campuses established on or after , by medical schools with “Full Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education) or “Accreditation” status (as such term is defined by the American Osteopathic Association Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation).January 1, 2000

children.label: IV
children.text: Hospitals that serve areas designated as health professional shortage areas under , as determined by the Secretary.section 254e(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Pro rata application
children.label: iii Pro rata application
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that each qualifying hospital that submits a timely application under subparagraph (A) receives at least 1 (or a fraction of 1) of the positions made available under this paragraph before any qualifying hospital receives more than 1 of such positions.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: i Limitation
children.text: A hospital may not receive more than 10 additional full-time equivalent residency positions under this paragraph.

children.heading: Prohibition on distribution to hospitals without an increase agreement
children.label: ii Prohibition on distribution to hospitals without an increase agreement
children.text: No increase in the otherwise applicable resident limit of a hospital may be made under this paragraph unless such hospital agrees to increase the total number of full-time equivalent residency positions under the approved medical residency training program of such hospital by the number of such positions made available by such increase under this paragraph.

children.heading: Requirement for hospitals to expand programs
children.label: iii Requirement for hospitals to expand programs
children.text: If a hospital that receives an increase in the otherwise applicable resident limit under this paragraph would be eligible for an adjustment to the otherwise applicable resident limit for participation in a new medical residency training program under section 413.79(e)(3) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation), the hospital shall ensure that any positions made available under this paragraph are used to expand an existing program of the hospital, and not for participation in a new medical residency training program.

children.heading: Application of per resident amounts for nonprimary care
children.label: D Application of per resident amounts for nonprimary care
children.text: With respect to additional residency positions in a hospital attributable to the increase provided under this paragraph, the approved FTE per resident amounts are deemed to be equal to the hospital per resident amounts for nonprimary care computed under paragraph (2)(D) for that hospital.

children.heading: Permitting facilities to apply aggregation rules
children.label: E Permitting facilities to apply aggregation rules
children.text: The Secretary shall permit hospitals receiving additional residency positions attributable to the increase provided under this paragraph to, beginning in the fifth year after the effective date of such increase, apply such positions to the limitation amount under paragraph (4)(F) that may be aggregated pursuant to paragraph (4)(H) among members of the same affiliated group.

children.heading: Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.label: i Otherwise applicable resident limit
children.text: The term “otherwise applicable resident limit” means, with respect to a hospital, the limit otherwise applicable under subparagraphs (F)(i) and (H) of paragraph (4) on the resident level for the hospital determined without regard to this paragraph but taking into account paragraphs (7)(A), (7)(B), (8)(A), (8)(B), and (9)(A).

children.heading: Psychiatry or psychiatry subspecialty residency
children.label: ii Psychiatry or psychiatry subspecialty residency
children.text: The term “psychiatry or psychiatry subspecialty residency” means a residency in psychiatry as accredited by the Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education for the purpose of preventing, diagnosing, and treating mental health disorders.

children.heading: Qualifying hospital
children.label: iii Qualifying hospital
children.text: The term “qualifying hospital” means a hospital described in any of subclauses (I) through (IV) of subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: Reference resident level
children.label: iv Reference resident level
children.text: The term “reference resident level” means, with respect to a hospital, the resident level for the most recent cost reporting period of the hospital ending on or before , for which a cost report has been settled (or, if not, submitted (subject to audit)), as determined by the Secretary.December 29, 2022

children.heading: Resident level
children.label: v Resident level
children.text: The term “resident level” has the meaning given such term in paragraph (7)(C)(i).

children.heading: Avoiding duplicative payments to hospitals participating in rural demonstration programs
children.label: i Avoiding duplicative payments to hospitals participating in rural demonstration programs
children.text: The Secretary shall reduce any payment amounts otherwise determined under this section to the extent necessary to avoid duplication of any payment made under section 4005(e) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1987.

children.heading: Prospective payment for inpatient rehabilitation services
children.label: j Prospective payment for inpatient rehabilitation services
i
children.text: the TEFRA percentage (as defined in subparagraph (C)) of the amount that would have been paid under part A with respect to such costs if this subsection did not apply, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the prospective payment percentage (as defined in subparagraph (C)) of the product of (I) the per unit payment rate established under this subsection for the fiscal year in which the payment unit of service occurs, and (II) the number of such payment units occurring in the cost reporting period.

children.heading: Fully implemented system
children.label: B Fully implemented system
children.text: Notwithstanding , but subject to the provisions of , the amount of the payment with respect to the operating and capital costs of inpatient hospital services of a rehabilitation facility for a payment unit in a cost reporting period beginning on or after , or, in the case of a facility making an election under subparagraph (F), for any cost reporting period described in such subparagraph, is equal to the per unit payment rate established under this subsection for the fiscal year in which the payment unit of service occurs.section 1395f(b) of this titlesection 1395e of this titleOctober 1, 2002

children.heading: TEFRA and prospective payment percentages specified
children.label: C TEFRA and prospective payment percentages specified
i
children.text: on or after , and before , the “TEFRA percentage” is 66⅔ percent and the “prospective payment percentage” is 33⅓ percent; andOctober 1, 2000October 1, 2001

children.label: ii
children.text: on or after , and before , the “TEFRA percentage” is 33⅓ percent and the “prospective payment percentage” is 66⅔ percent.October 1, 2001October 1, 2002

children.heading: Payment unit
children.label: D Payment unit
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “payment unit” means a discharge.

children.heading: Construction relating to transfer authority
children.label: E Construction relating to transfer authority
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as preventing the Secretary from providing for an adjustment to payments to take into account the early transfer of a patient from a rehabilitation facility to another site of care.

children.heading: Election to apply full prospective payment system
children.label: F Election to apply full prospective payment system
children.text: A rehabilitation facility may elect, not later than 30 days before its first cost reporting period for which the payment methodology under this subsection applies to the facility, to have payment made to the facility under this subsection under the provisions of subparagraph (B) (rather than subparagraph (A)) for each cost reporting period to which such payment methodology applies.

children.label: i
children.text: classes of patient discharges of rehabilitation facilities by functional-related groups (each in this subsection referred to as a “case mix group”), based on impairment, age, comorbidities, and functional capability of the patient and such other factors as the Secretary deems appropriate to improve the explanatory power of functional independence measure-function related groups; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a method of classifying specific patients in rehabilitation facilities within these groups.

children.heading: Weighting factors
children.label: B Weighting factors
children.text: For each case mix group the Secretary shall assign an appropriate weighting which reflects the relative facility resources used with respect to patients classified within that group compared to patients classified within other groups.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall from time to time adjust the classifications and weighting factors established under this paragraph as appropriate to reflect changes in treatment patterns, technology, case mix, number of payment units for which payment is made under this subchapter, and other factors which may affect the relative use of resources. Such adjustments shall be made in a manner so that changes in aggregate payments under the classification system are a result of real changes and are not a result of changes in coding that are unrelated to real changes in case mix.

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: ii Adjustment
children.text: Insofar as the Secretary determines that such adjustments for a previous fiscal year (or estimates that such adjustments for a future fiscal year) did (or are likely to) result in a change in aggregate payments under the classification system during the fiscal year that are a result of changes in the coding or classification of patients that do not reflect real changes in case mix, the Secretary shall adjust the per payment unit payment rate for subsequent years so as to eliminate the effect of such coding or classification changes.

children.heading: Data collection
children.label: D Data collection
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to require rehabilitation facilities that provide inpatient hospital services to submit such data as the Secretary deems necessary to establish and administer the prospective payment system under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: by updating such per-payment-unit amount to the fiscal year involved by the weighted average of the applicable percentage increases provided under subsection (b)(3)(B)(ii) (for cost reporting periods beginning during the fiscal year) covering the period from the midpoint of the period for such data through the midpoint of fiscal year 2000 and by an increase factor (described in subparagraph (C)) specified by the Secretary for subsequent fiscal years up to the fiscal year involved;

children.label: ii
children.text: by reducing such rates by a factor equal to the proportion of payments under this subsection (as estimated by the Secretary) based on prospective payment amounts which are additional payments described in paragraph (4) (relating to outlier and related payments);

children.label: iii
children.text: for variations among rehabilitation facilities by area under paragraph (6);

children.label: iv
children.text: by the weighting factors established under paragraph (2)(B); and

children.label: v
children.text: by such other factors as the Secretary determines are necessary to properly reflect variations in necessary costs of treatment among rehabilitation facilities.

children.heading: Budget neutral rates
children.label: B Budget neutral rates
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the prospective payment amounts under this subsection for payment units during fiscal years 2001 and 2002 at levels such that, in the Secretary’s estimation, the amount of total payments under this subsection for such fiscal years (including any payment adjustments pursuant to paragraphs (4) and (6) but not taking into account any payment adjustment resulting from an election permitted under paragraph (1)(F)) shall be equal to 98 percent for fiscal year 2001 and 100 percent for fiscal year 2002 of the amount of payments that would have been made under this subchapter during the fiscal years for operating and capital costs of rehabilitation facilities had this subsection not been enacted. In establishing such payment amounts, the Secretary shall consider the effects of the prospective payment system established under this subsection on the total number of payment units from rehabilitation facilities and other factors described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection for payment units in each fiscal year (beginning with fiscal year 2001), the Secretary shall establish an increase factor subject to clauses (ii) and (iii). Such factor shall be based on an appropriate percentage increase in a market basket of goods and services comprising services for which payment is made under this subsection, which may be the market basket percentage increase described in subsection (b)(3)(B)(iii). The increase factor to be applied under this subparagraph for each of fiscal years 2008 and 2009 shall be 0 percent.

children.label: I
children.text: for fiscal year 2012 and each subsequent fiscal year, by the productivity adjustment described in subsection (b)(3)(B)(xi)(II); and

children.label: II
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2019, by the other adjustment described in subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Special rule for fiscal year 2018
children.label: iii Special rule for fiscal year 2018
children.text: The increase factor to be applied under this subparagraph for fiscal year 2018, after the application of clause (ii), shall be 1 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2010 and 2011, 0.25 percentage point;

children.label: ii
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2012 and 2013, 0.1 percentage point;

children.label: iii
children.text: for fiscal year 2014, 0.3 percentage point;

children.label: iv
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2015 and 2016, 0.2 percentage point; and

children.label: v
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2017, 2018, and 2019, 0.75 percentage point.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide for an additional payment to a rehabilitation facility for patients in a case mix group, based upon the patient being classified as an outlier based on an unusual length of stay, costs, or other factors specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Payment based on marginal cost of care
children.label: ii Payment based on marginal cost of care
children.text: The amount of such additional payment under clause (i) shall be determined by the Secretary and shall approximate the marginal cost of care beyond the cutoff point applicable under clause (i).

children.heading: Total payments
children.label: iii Total payments
children.text: The total amount of the additional payments made under this subparagraph for payment units in a fiscal year may not exceed 5 percent of the total payments projected or estimated to be made based on prospective payment rates for payment units in that year.

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: B Adjustment
children.text: The Secretary may provide for such adjustments to the payment amounts under this subsection as the Secretary deems appropriate to take into account the unique circumstances of rehabilitation facilities located in Alaska and Hawaii.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 5 Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for publication in the Federal Register, on or before August 1 before each fiscal year (beginning with fiscal year 2001), of the classification and weighting factors for case mix groups under paragraph (2) for such fiscal year and a description of the methodology and data used in computing the prospective payment rates under this subsection for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Area wage adjustment
children.label: 6 Area wage adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust the proportion (as estimated by the Secretary from time to time) of rehabilitation facilities’ costs which are attributable to wages and wage-related costs, of the prospective payment rates computed under paragraph (3) for area differences in wage levels by a factor (established by the Secretary) reflecting the relative hospital wage level in the geographic area of the rehabilitation facility compared to the national average wage level for such facilities. Not later than (and at least every 36 months thereafter), the Secretary shall update the factor under the preceding sentence on the basis of information available to the Secretary (and updated as appropriate) of the wages and wage-related costs incurred in furnishing rehabilitation services. Any adjustments or updates made under this paragraph for a fiscal year shall be made in a manner that assures that the aggregated payments under this subsection in the fiscal year are not greater or less than those that would have been made in the year without such adjustment.October 1, 2001

children.heading: Reduction in update for failure to report
children.label: A Reduction in update for failure to report

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of fiscal year 2014 and each subsequent fiscal year, in the case of a rehabilitation facility that does not submit data to the Secretary in accordance with subparagraphs (C) and (F) with respect to such a fiscal year, after determining the increase factor described in paragraph (3)(C), and after application of subparagraphs (C)(iii) and (D) of paragraph (3), the Secretary shall reduce such increase factor for payments for discharges occurring during such fiscal year by 2 percentage points.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: The application of this subparagraph may result in the increase factor described in paragraph (3)(C) being less than 0.0 for a fiscal year, and may result in payment rates under this subsection for a fiscal year being less than such payment rates for the preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Noncumulative application
children.label: B Noncumulative application
children.text: Any reduction under subparagraph (A) shall apply only with respect to the fiscal year involved and the Secretary shall not take into account such reduction in computing the payment amount under this subsection for a subsequent fiscal year.

children.heading: Submission of quality data
children.label: C Submission of quality data
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (G), for fiscal year 2014 and each subsequent fiscal year, each rehabilitation facility shall submit to the Secretary data on quality measures specified under subparagraph (D). Such data shall be submitted in a form and manner, and at a time, specified by the Secretary for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), any measure specified by the Secretary under this subparagraph must have been endorsed by the entity with a contract under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: In the case of a specified area or medical topic determined appropriate by the Secretary for which a feasible and practical measure has not been endorsed by the entity with a contract under , the Secretary may specify a measure that is not so endorsed as long as due consideration is given to measures that have been endorsed or adopted by a consensus organization identified by the Secretary.section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Time frame
children.label: iii Time frame
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall publish the measures selected under this subparagraph that will be applicable with respect to fiscal year 2014.October 1, 2012

children.heading: Public availability of data submitted
children.label: E Public availability of data submitted

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
