children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures for making data submitted under subparagraph (C) and subparagraph (F)(i) available to the public. Such procedures shall ensure that a rehabilitation facility has the opportunity to review the data that is to be made public with respect to the facility prior to such data being made public. The Secretary shall report quality measures that relate to services furnished in inpatient settings in rehabilitation facilities on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.heading: Public recognition of rehabilitation innovation centers
children.label: ii Public recognition of rehabilitation innovation centers
children.text: Beginning not later than 18 months after , the Secretary shall make publicly available on such Internet website, in addition to the information required to be reported on such website under clause (i), a list of all rehabilitation innovation centers, and shall update such list on such website not less frequently than biennially.January 5, 2023

children.heading: Rehabilitation innovation centers defined
children.label: iii Rehabilitation innovation centers defined
children.text: For purposes of clause (ii), the term “rehabilitation innovation centers” means a rehabilitation facility that, as of the applicable date (as defined in clause (v)), is a rehabilitation facility described in clause (iv).

children.label: aa
children.text: is classified as a rehabilitation facility under the IRF Rate Setting File for the Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility Prospective Payment System for Federal Fiscal Year 2019 (83 Fed. Reg. 38514), or any successor regulations that contain such information;

children.label: bb
children.text: holds at least one Federal rehabilitation research and training designation for research projects on traumatic brain injury or spinal cord injury from the National Institute on Disability, Independent Living, and Rehabilitation Research at the Department of Health and Human Services, based on such data submitted to the Secretary by a facility, in a form, manner, and time frame specified by the Secretary;

children.label: cc
children.text: submits to the Secretary a description of the clinical research enterprise of the facility and a summary of research activities of the facility that are supported by Federal agencies;

children.label: dd
children.text: has a minimum Medicare estimated average weight per discharge of 1.20 for the most recent fiscal year for which such information is available according to the IRF Rate Setting File described in item (aa), or any successor regulations that contain such information; and

children.label: ee
children.text: has a minimum teaching status of 0.075 for the most recent fiscal year for which such information is available according to the IRF Rate Setting File described in item (aa), or any successor regulations that contain such information.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: II Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may, as determined appropriate, waive any of the requirements under items (aa) through (ee) of subclause (I).

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to the initial publication of a list under clause (ii), ; andJanuary 5, 2023

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to the publication of an updated list under clause (ii), a date specified by the Secretary that is not more than one year prior to the date of such publication.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: vi Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law the Secretary may implement clauses (ii) through (v) by program instruction or otherwise.

children.heading: Nonapplication of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.label: vii Nonapplication of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to data collected under clauses (ii) through (v).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For the fiscal year beginning on the specified application date (as defined in subsection (a)(2)(E) of section 1395 of this title), as applicable with respect to inpatient rehabilitation facilities and quality measures under subsection (c)(1) of such section and measures under subsection (d)(1) of such section, and each subsequent fiscal year, in addition to such data on the quality measures described in subparagraph (C), each rehabilitation facility shall submit to the Secretary data on the quality measures under such subsection (c)(1) and any necessary data specified by the Secretary under such subsection (d)(1).lll

children.heading: Standardized patient assessment data
children.label: ii Standardized patient assessment data
children.text: For fiscal year 2019 and each subsequent fiscal year, in addition to such data described in clause (i), each rehabilitation facility shall submit to the Secretary standardized patient assessment data required under subsection (b)(1) of section 1395 of this title.lll

children.heading: Submission
children.label: iii Submission
children.text: Such data shall be submitted in the form and manner, and at the time, specified by the Secretary for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Non-duplication
children.label: G Non-duplication
children.text: To the extent data submitted under subparagraph (F) duplicates other data required to be submitted under subparagraph (C), the submission of such data under subparagraph (F) shall be in lieu of the submission of such data under subparagraph (C). The previous sentence shall not apply insofar as the Secretary determines it is necessary to avoid a delay in the implementation of section 1395 of this title, taking into account the different specified application dates under subsection (a)(2)(E) of such section.lll

children.label: A
children.text: case mix groups, of the methodology for the classification of patients within such groups, and of the appropriate weighting factors thereof under paragraph (2),

children.label: B
children.text: the prospective payment rates under paragraph (3),

children.label: C
children.text: outlier and special payments under paragraph (4), and

children.label: D
children.text: area wage adjustments under paragraph (6).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For cost reporting periods beginning on or after , the Secretary may establish rules for payment to qualified nonhospital providers for their direct costs of medical education, if those costs are incurred in the operation of an approved medical residency training program described in subsection (h). Such rules shall specify the amounts, form, and manner in which such payments will be made and the portion of such payments that will be made from each of the trust funds under this subchapter.October 1, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: a Federally qualified health center, as defined in ;15section 1395x(aa)(4) of this titleSo in original. Probably should not be capitalized.15

children.label: B
children.text: a rural health clinic, as defined in ;section 1395x(aa)(2) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Medicare+Choice organizations; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other providers (other than hospitals) as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Payment for nursing and allied health education for managed care enrollees
children.label: l Payment for nursing and allied health education for managed care enrollees

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For portions of cost reporting periods occurring in a year (beginning with 2000), the Secretary shall provide for an additional payment amount for any hospital that receives payments for the costs of approved educational activities for nurse and allied health professional training under .section 1395x(v)(1) of this title

children.heading: Determination of managed care enrollee payment ratio for graduate medical education payments
children.label: A Determination of managed care enrollee payment ratio for graduate medical education payments
children.text: The Secretary shall estimate the ratio of payments for all hospitals for portions of cost reporting periods occurring in the year under subsection (h)(3)(D) to total direct graduate medical education payments estimated for such portions of periods under subsection (h)(3).

children.heading: Application to fee-for-service nursing and allied health education payments
children.label: B Application to fee-for-service nursing and allied health education payments

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), such ratio shall be applied to the Secretary’s estimate of total payments for nursing and allied health education determined under for portions of cost reporting periods occurring in the year to determine a total amount of additional payments for nursing and allied health education to be distributed to hospitals under this subsection for portions of cost reporting periods occurring in the year; except that in no case shall such total amount exceed $60,000,000 in any year.section 1395x(v) of this title

children.heading: Exception to annual limitation for each of 2010 through 2019
children.label: ii Exception to annual limitation for each of 2010 through 2019
children.text: For each of 2010 through 2019, the limitation under clause (i) on the total amount of additional payments for nursing and allied health education to be distributed to hospitals under this subsection for portions of cost reporting periods occurring in the year shall not apply to such payments made in such year to those hospitals that, as of , are operating a school of nursing, a school of allied health, or a school of nursing and allied health.December 29, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: the product of (I) the Secretary’s estimate of the ratio of the amount of payments made under to the hospital for nursing and allied health education activities for the hospital’s cost reporting period ending in the second preceding fiscal year, to the hospital’s total inpatient days for such period, and (II) the total number of inpatient days (as established by the Secretary) for such period which are attributable to services furnished to individuals who are enrolled under a risk sharing contract with an eligible organization under and who are entitled to benefits under part A or who are enrolled with a Medicare+Choice organization under part C; tosection 1395x(v) of this titlesection 1395mm of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the sum of the products determined under clause (i) for such cost reporting periods.

children.heading: Prospective payment for long-term care hospitals
children.label: m Prospective payment for long-term care hospitals

children.heading: Reference to establishment and implementation of system
children.label: 1 Reference to establishment and implementation of system
children.text: For provisions related to the establishment and implementation of a prospective payment system for payments under this subchapter for inpatient hospital services furnished by a long-term care hospital described in subsection (d)(1)(B)(iv), see section 123 of the Medicare, Medicaid, and SCHIP Balanced Budget Refinement Act of 1999 and section 307(b) of the Medicare, Medicaid, and SCHIP Benefits Improvement and Protection Act of 2000.

children.heading: Update for rate year 2008
children.label: 2 Update for rate year 2008
children.text: In implementing the system described in paragraph (1) for discharges occurring during the rate year ending in 2008 for a hospital, the base rate for such discharges for the hospital shall be the same as the base rate for discharges for the hospital occurring during the rate year ending in 2007.

children.heading: Implementation for rate year 2010 and subsequent years
children.label: 3 Implementation for rate year 2010 and subsequent years
i
children.text: for rate year 2012 and each subsequent rate year, by the productivity adjustment described in subsection (b)(3)(B)(xi)(II); and

children.label: ii
children.text: for each of rate years 2010 through 2019, by the other adjustment described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: The application of this paragraph may result in such annual update being less than 0.0 for a rate year, and may result in payment rates under the system described in paragraph (1) for a rate year being less than such payment rates for the preceding rate year.

children.heading: Additional special rule
children.label: C Additional special rule
children.text: For fiscal year 2018, the annual update under subparagraph (A) for the fiscal year, after application of clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A), shall be 1 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: for rate year 2010, 0.25 percentage point;

children.label: B
children.text: for rate year 2011, 0.50 percentage point;

children.label: C
children.text: for each of the rate years beginning in 2012 and 2013, 0.1 percentage point;

children.label: D
children.text: for rate year 2014, 0.3 percentage point;

children.label: E
children.text: for each of rate years 2015 and 2016, 0.2 percentage point; and

children.label: F
children.text: for each of rate years 2017, 2018, and 2019, 0.75 percentage point.

children.heading: Reduction in update for failure to report
children.label: A Reduction in update for failure to report

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Under the system described in paragraph (1), for rate year 2014 and each subsequent rate year, in the case of a long-term care hospital that does not submit data to the Secretary in accordance with subparagraphs (C) and (F) with respect to such a rate year, any annual update to a standard Federal rate for discharges for the hospital during the rate year, and after application of paragraph (3), shall be reduced by 2 percentage points.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: The application of this subparagraph may result in such annual update being less than 0.0 for a rate year, and may result in payment rates under the system described in paragraph (1) for a rate year being less than such payment rates for the preceding rate year.

children.heading: Noncumulative application
children.label: B Noncumulative application
children.text: Any reduction under subparagraph (A) shall apply only with respect to the rate year involved and the Secretary shall not take into account such reduction in computing the payment amount under the system described in paragraph (1) for a subsequent rate year.

children.heading: Submission of quality data
children.label: C Submission of quality data
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (G), for rate year 2014 and each subsequent rate year, each long-term care hospital shall submit to the Secretary data on quality measures specified under subparagraph (D). Such data shall be submitted in a form and manner, and at a time, specified by the Secretary for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), any measure specified by the Secretary under this subparagraph must have been endorsed by the entity with a contract under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: In the case of a specified area or medical topic determined appropriate by the Secretary for which a feasible and practical measure has not been endorsed by the entity with a contract under , the Secretary may specify a measure that is not so endorsed as long as due consideration is given to measures that have been endorsed or adopted by a consensus organization identified by the Secretary.section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Time frame
children.label: iii Time frame
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall publish the measures selected under this subparagraph that will be applicable with respect to rate year 2014.October 1, 2012

children.heading: Additional quality measures
children.label: iv Additional quality measures
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish a functional status quality measure for change in mobility among inpatients requiring ventilator support.October 1, 2015

children.heading: Public availability of data submitted
children.label: E Public availability of data submitted
children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures for making data submitted under subparagraph (C) and subparagraph (F)(i) available to the public. Such procedures shall ensure that a long-term care hospital has the opportunity to review the data that is to be made public with respect to the hospital prior to such data being made public. The Secretary shall report quality measures that relate to services furnished in inpatient settings in long-term care hospitals on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For the rate year beginning on the specified application date (as defined in subsection (a)(2)(E) of section 1395 of this title), as applicable with respect to long-term care hospitals and quality measures under subsection (c)(1) of such section and measures under subsection (d)(1) of such section, and each subsequent rate year, in addition to the data on the quality measures described in subparagraph (C), each long-term care hospital (other than a hospital classified under subsection (d)(1)(B)(vi)) shall submit to the Secretary data on the quality measures under such subsection (c)(1) and any necessary data specified by the Secretary under such subsection (d)(1).lll

children.heading: Standardized patient assessment data
children.label: ii Standardized patient assessment data
children.text: For rate year 2019 and each subsequent rate year, in addition to such data described in clause (i), each long-term care hospital (other than a hospital classified under subsection (d)(1)(B)(vi)) shall submit to the Secretary standardized patient assessment data required under subsection (b)(1) of section 1395 of this title.lll

children.heading: Submission
children.label: iii Submission
children.text: Such data shall be submitted in the form and manner, and at the time, specified by the Secretary for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Non-duplication
children.label: G Non-duplication
children.text: To the extent data submitted under subparagraph (F) duplicates other data required to be submitted under subparagraph (C), the submission of such data under subparagraph (F) shall be in lieu of the submission of such data under subparagraph (C). The previous sentence shall not apply insofar as the Secretary determines it is necessary to avoid a delay in the implementation of section 1395 of this title, taking into account the different specified application dates under subsection (a)(2)(E) of such section.lll

children.heading: Application of site neutral IPPS payment rate in certain cases
children.label: 6 Application of site neutral IPPS payment rate in certain cases

children.heading: General application of site neutral IPPS payment amount for discharges failing to meet applicable criteria
children.label: A General application of site neutral IPPS payment amount for discharges failing to meet applicable criteria

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For a discharge in cost reporting periods beginning on or after , except as provided in clause (ii) and subparagraphs (C), (E), (F), and (G), payment under this subchapter to a long-term care hospital for inpatient hospital services shall be made at the applicable site neutral payment rate (as defined in subparagraph (B)).October 1, 2015

children.heading: Exception for certain discharges meeting criteria
children.label: ii Exception for certain discharges meeting criteria
I
children.text: the discharge meets the ICU criterion under clause (iii) or the ventilator criterion under clause (iv); and

children.label: II
children.text: the discharge does not have a principal diagnosis relating to a psychiatric diagnosis or to rehabilitation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The criterion specified in this clause (in this paragraph referred to as the “ICU criterion”), for a discharge from a long-term care hospital, is that the stay in the long-term care hospital ending with such discharge was immediately preceded by a discharge from a stay in a subsection (d) hospital that included at least 3 days in an intensive care unit (ICU), as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Determining ICU days
children.label: II Determining ICU days
children.text: In determining intensive care unit days under subclause (I), the Secretary shall use data from revenue center codes 020x or 021x (or such successor codes as the Secretary may establish).

children.label: I
children.text: the stay in the long-term care hospital ending with such discharge was immediately preceded by a discharge from a stay in a subsection (d) hospital; and

children.label: II
children.text: the individual discharged was assigned to a Medicare-Severity-Long-Term-Care-Diagnosis-Related-Group (MS–LTC–DRG) based on the receipt of ventilator services of at least 96 hours.

children.heading: Applicable site neutral payment rate defined
children.label: B Applicable site neutral payment rate defined
I
children.text: for discharges in cost reporting periods beginning during fiscal years 2016 through 2019, the blended payment rate specified in clause (iii); and

children.label: II
children.text: for discharges in cost reporting periods beginning during fiscal year 2020 or a subsequent fiscal year, the site neutral payment rate (as defined in clause (ii)).

children.heading: Site neutral payment rate defined
children.label: ii Site neutral payment rate defined
I
children.text: the IPPS comparable per diem amount determined under paragraph (d)(4) of section 412.529 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, including any applicable outlier payments under section 412.525 of such title; or

children.label: II
children.text: 100 percent of the estimated cost for the services involved.

children.label: I
children.text: half of the site neutral payment rate (as defined in clause (ii)) for the discharge; and

children.label: II
children.text: half of the payment rate that would otherwise be applicable to such discharge without regard to this paragraph, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: iv Adjustment
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2018 through 2026, the amount that would otherwise apply under clause (ii)(I) for the year (determined without regard to this clause) shall be reduced by 4.6 percent.

children.heading: Limiting payment for all hospital discharges to site neutral payment rate for hospitals failing to meet applicable LTCH discharge thresholds
children.label: C Limiting payment for all hospital discharges to site neutral payment rate for hospitals failing to meet applicable LTCH discharge thresholds

children.heading: Notice of LTCH discharge payment percentage
children.label: i Notice of LTCH discharge payment percentage
children.text: For cost reporting periods beginning during or after fiscal year 2016, the Secretary shall inform each long-term care hospital of its LTCH discharge payment percentage (as defined in clause (iv)) for such period.

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary shall inform the hospital of such fact; and

children.label: II
children.text: subject to clause (iii), for all discharges in the hospital in each succeeding cost reporting period, the payment amount under this subsection shall be the payment amount that would apply under subsection (d) for the discharge if the hospital were a subsection (d) hospital.

children.heading: Process for reinstatement
children.label: iii Process for reinstatement
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process whereby a long-term care hospital may seek to and have the provisions of subclause (II) of clause (ii) discontinued with respect to that hospital.

children.label: I
children.text: the number of Medicare fee-for-service discharges for such hospital and period for which payment is not made at the site neutral payment rate, to

children.label: II
children.text: the total number of Medicare fee-for-service discharges for such hospital and period.

children.heading: Inclusion of subsection (d) Puerto Rico hospitals
children.label: D Inclusion of subsection (d) Puerto Rico hospitals
children.text: In this paragraph, any reference in this paragraph to a subsection (d) hospital shall be deemed to include a reference to a subsection (d) Puerto Rico hospital.

children.heading: Temporary exception for certain severe wound discharges from certain long-term care hospitals
children.label: E Temporary exception for certain severe wound discharges from certain long-term care hospitals
aa
children.text: identified by the last sentence of subsection (d)(1)(B); and

children.label: bb
children.text: located in a rural area (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)) or treated as being so located pursuant to subsection (d)(8)(E); and

children.label: II
children.text: the individual discharged has a severe wound.

children.heading: Severe wound defined
children.label: ii Severe wound defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “severe wound” means a stage 3 wound, stage 4 wound, unstageable wound, non-healing surgical wound, infected wound, fistula, osteomyelitis, or wound with morbid obesity, as identified in the claim from the long-term care hospital.

children.heading: Temporary exception for certain spinal cord specialty hospitals
children.label: F Temporary exception for certain spinal cord specialty hospitals

children.heading: Not-for-profit
children.label: i Not-for-profit
children.text: The long-term care hospital was a not-for-profit long-term care hospital on , as determined by cost report data.June 1, 2014

children.heading: Primarily providing treatment for catastrophic spinal cord or acquired brain injuries or other paralyzing neuromuscular conditions
children.label: ii Primarily providing treatment for catastrophic spinal cord or acquired brain injuries or other paralyzing neuromuscular conditions
children.text: Of the discharges in calendar year 2013 from the long-term care hospital for which payment was made under this section, at least 50 percent were classified under MS–LTCH–DRGs 28, 29, 52, 57, 551, 573, and 963.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The long-term care hospital discharged inpatients (including both individuals entitled to, or enrolled for, benefits under this subchapter and individuals not so entitled or enrolled) during fiscal year 2014 who had been admitted from at least 20 of the 50 States, determined by the States of residency of such inpatients and based on such data submitted by the hospital to the Secretary as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: II Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement subclause (I) by program instruction or otherwise.

children.heading: Non-application of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.label: III Non-application of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to data collected under this clause.

children.heading: Additional temporary exception for certain severe wound discharges from certain long-term care hospitals
children.label: G Additional temporary exception for certain severe wound discharges from certain long-term care hospitals
I
children.text: is from a long-term care hospital identified by the last sentence of subsection (d)(1)(B);

children.label: II
children.text: is classified under MS–LTCH–DRG 602, 603, 539, or 540; and

children.label: III
children.text: is with respect to an individual treated by a long-term care hospital for a severe wound.

children.heading: Severe wound defined
children.label: ii Severe wound defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “severe wound” means a wound which is a stage 3 wound, stage 4 wound, unstageable wound, non-healing surgical wound, or fistula as identified in the claim from the long-term care hospital.

children.heading: Wound defined
children.label: iii Wound defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “wound” means an injury involving division of tissue or rupture of the integument or mucous membrane with exposure to the external environment.

children.heading: Treatment of high cost outlier payments
children.label: 7 Treatment of high cost outlier payments

children.heading: Adjustment to the standard Federal payment rate for estimated high cost outlier payments
children.label: A Adjustment to the standard Federal payment rate for estimated high cost outlier payments
children.text: Under the system described in paragraph (1), for fiscal years beginning on or after , the Secretary shall reduce the standard Federal payment rate as if the estimated aggregate amount of high cost outlier payments for standard Federal payment rate discharges for each such fiscal year would be equal to 8 percent of estimated aggregate payments for standard Federal payment rate discharges for each such fiscal year.October 1, 2017

children.heading: Limitation on high cost outlier payment amounts
children.label: B Limitation on high cost outlier payment amounts
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall set the fixed loss amount for high cost outlier payments such that the estimated aggregate amount of high cost outlier payments made for standard Federal payment rate discharges for fiscal years beginning on or after , shall be equal to 99.6875 percent of 8 percent of estimated aggregate payments for standard Federal payment rate discharges for each such fiscal year.October 1, 2017

children.heading: Waiver of budget neutrality
children.label: C Waiver of budget neutrality
children.text: Any reduction in payments resulting from the application of subparagraph (B) shall not be taken into account in applying any budget neutrality provision under such system.

children.heading: No effect on site neutral high cost outlier payment rate
children.label: D No effect on site neutral high cost outlier payment rate
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply with respect to the computation of the applicable site neutral payment rate under paragraph (6).

children.heading: Incentives for adoption and meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.label: n Incentives for adoption and meaningful use of certified EHR technology

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding provisions of this subsection, with respect to inpatient hospital services furnished by an eligible hospital during a payment year (as defined in paragraph (2)(G)), if the eligible hospital is a meaningful EHR user (as determined under paragraph (3)) for the EHR reporting period with respect to such year, in addition to the amount otherwise paid under this section, there also shall be paid to the eligible hospital, from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund established under , an amount equal to the applicable amount specified in paragraph (2)(A) for the hospital for such payment year.section 1395i of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the base amount specified in subparagraph (B); plus

children.label: II
children.text: the discharge related amount specified in subparagraph (C) for a 12-month period selected by the Secretary with respect to such payment year.

children.heading: Medicare share
children.label: ii Medicare share
children.text: The Medicare share as specified in subparagraph (D) for the eligible hospital for a period selected by the Secretary with respect to such payment year.

children.heading: Transition factor
children.label: iii Transition factor
children.text: The transition factor specified in subparagraph (E) for the eligible hospital for the payment year.

children.heading: Base amount
children.label: B Base amount
children.text: The base amount specified in this subparagraph is $2,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: For the first through 1,149th discharge, $0.

children.label: ii
children.text: For the 1,150th through the 23,000th discharge, $200.

children.label: iii
children.text: For any discharge greater than the 23,000th, $0.

children.label: I
children.text: the estimated number of inpatient-bed-days (as established by the Secretary) which are attributable to individuals with respect to whom payment may be made under part A; and

children.label: II
children.text: the estimated number of inpatient-bed-days (as so established) which are attributable to individuals who are enrolled with a Medicare Advantage organization under part C; and

children.label: I
children.text: the estimated total number of inpatient-bed-days with respect to the eligible hospital during such period; and

children.label: II
children.text: the estimated total amount of the eligible hospital’s charges during such period, not including any charges that are attributable to charity care (as such term is used for purposes of hospital cost reporting under this subchapter), divided by the estimated total amount of the hospital’s charges during such period.

children.label: I
children.text: For the first payment year for such hospital, 1.

children.label: II
children.text: For the second payment year for such hospital, ¾.

children.label: III
children.text: For the third payment year for such hospital, ½.

children.label: IV
children.text: For the fourth payment year for such hospital, ¼.

children.label: V
children.text: For any succeeding payment year for such hospital, 0.

children.heading: Phase down for eligible hospitals first adopting EHR after 2013
children.label: ii Phase down for eligible hospitals first adopting EHR after 2013
children.text: If the first payment year for an eligible hospital is after 2013, then the transition factor specified in this subparagraph for a payment year for such hospital is the same as the amount specified in clause (i) for such payment year for an eligible hospital for which the first payment year is 2013. If the first payment year for an eligible hospital is after 2015 then the transition factor specified in this subparagraph for such hospital and for such year and any subsequent year shall be 0.

children.heading: Form of payment
children.label: F Form of payment
children.text: The payment under this subsection for a payment year may be in the form of a single consolidated payment or in the form of such periodic installments as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “payment year” means a fiscal year beginning with fiscal year 2011.

children.heading: First, second, etc. payment year
children.label: ii First, second, etc. payment year
children.text: The term “first payment year” means, with respect to inpatient hospital services furnished by an eligible hospital, the first fiscal year for which an incentive payment is made for such services under this subsection. The terms “second payment year”, “third payment year”, and “fourth payment year” mean, with respect to an eligible hospital, each successive year immediately following the first payment year for that hospital.

children.heading: Meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.label: i Meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.text: The eligible hospital demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary, in accordance with subparagraph (C)(i), that during such period the hospital is using certified EHR technology in a meaningful manner.

children.heading: Information exchange
children.label: ii Information exchange
children.text: The eligible hospital demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary, in accordance with subparagraph (C)(i), that during such period such certified EHR technology is connected in a manner that provides, in accordance with law and standards applicable to the exchange of information, for the electronic exchange of health information to improve the quality of health care, such as promoting care coordination, and the hospital demonstrates (through a process specified by the Secretary, such as the use of an attestation) that the hospital has not knowingly and willfully taken action (such as to disable functionality) to limit or restrict the compatibility or interoperability of the certified EHR technology.

children.heading: Reporting on measures using EHR
children.label: iii Reporting on measures using EHR
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B)(ii) and using such certified EHR technology, the eligible hospital submits information for such period, in a form and manner specified by the Secretary, on such clinical quality measures and such other measures as selected by the Secretary under subparagraph (B)(i).

children.label: I
children.text: The Secretary shall provide preference to clinical quality measures that have been selected for purposes of applying subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii) or that have been endorsed by the entity with a contract with the Secretary under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: Prior to any measure (other than a clinical quality measure that has been selected for purposes of applying subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii)) being selected under this subparagraph, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register such measure and provide for a period of public comment on such measure.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: ii Limitations
children.text: The Secretary may not require the electronic reporting of information on clinical quality measures under subparagraph (A)(iii) unless the Secretary has the capacity to accept the information electronically, which may be on a pilot basis.

children.heading: Coordination of reporting of information
children.label: iii Coordination of reporting of information
children.text: In selecting such measures, and in establishing the form and manner for reporting measures under subparagraph (A)(iii), the Secretary shall seek to avoid redundant or duplicative reporting with reporting otherwise required, including reporting under subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii).

children.heading: Demonstration of meaningful use of certified EHR technology and information exchange
children.label: C Demonstration of meaningful use of certified EHR technology and information exchange
I
children.text: an attestation;

children.label: II
children.text: the submission of claims with appropriate coding (such as a code indicating that inpatient care was documented using certified EHR technology);

children.label: III
children.text: a survey response;

children.label: IV
children.text: reporting under subparagraph (A)(iii); and

children.label: V
children.text: other means specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Use of part D data
children.label: ii Use of part D data
children.text: Notwithstanding sections 1395w–115(d)(2)(B) and 1395w–115(f)(2) of this title, the Secretary may use data regarding drug claims submitted for purposes of that are necessary for purposes of subparagraph (A).section 1395w–115 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the methodology and standards for determining payment amounts under this subsection and payment adjustments under subsection (b)(3)(B)(ix), including selection of periods under paragraph (2) for determining, and making estimates or using proxies of, discharges under paragraph (2)(C) and inpatient-bed-days, hospital charges, charity charges, and Medicare share under paragraph (2)(D);

children.label: ii
children.text: the methodology and standards for determining a meaningful EHR user under paragraph (3), including selection of measures under paragraph (3)(B), specification of the means of demonstrating meaningful EHR use under paragraph (3)(C), and the hardship exception under subsection (b)(3)(B)(ix)(II); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the specification of EHR reporting periods under paragraph (6)(B) and the selection of the form of payment under paragraph (2)(F).16So in original. Probably should be “(6)(A)”.16

children.heading: Posting on website
children.label: B Posting on website
children.text: The Secretary shall post on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, in an easily understandable format, a list of the names of the eligible hospitals that are meaningful EHR users under this subsection or subsection (b)(3)(B)(ix) (and a list of the names of critical access hospitals to which paragraph (3) or (4) of section 1395f() of this title applies), and other relevant data as determined appropriate by the Secretary. The Secretary shall ensure that an eligible hospital (or critical access hospital) has the opportunity to review the other relevant data that are to be made public with respect to the hospital (or critical access hospital) prior to such data being made public.l

children.heading: Certified EHR technology defined
children.label: 5 Certified EHR technology defined
children.text: The term “certified EHR technology” has the meaning given such term in section 1395w–4()(4) of this title.o

children.heading: EHR reporting period
children.label: A EHR reporting period
children.text: The term “EHR reporting period” means, with respect to a payment year, any period (or periods) as specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Eligible hospital
children.label: B Eligible hospital
children.text: The term “eligible hospital” means a hospital that is a subsection (d) hospital or a subsection (d) Puerto Rico hospital.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding provisions of this subsection, the Secretary shall establish a hospital value-based purchasing program (in this subsection referred to as the “Program”) under which value-based incentive payments are made in a fiscal year to hospitals that meet the performance standards under paragraph (3) for the performance period for such fiscal year (as established under paragraph (4)).

children.heading: Program to begin in fiscal year 2013
children.label: B Program to begin in fiscal year 2013
children.text: The Program shall apply to payments for discharges occurring on or after .October 1, 2012

children.heading: Applicability of Program to hospitals
children.label: C Applicability of Program to hospitals

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, subject to clause (ii), the term “hospital” means a subsection (d) hospital (as defined in subsection (d)(1)(B)).

children.label: I
children.text: that is subject to the payment reduction under subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii)(I) for such fiscal year;

children.label: II
children.text: for which, during the performance period for such fiscal year, the Secretary has cited deficiencies that pose immediate jeopardy to the health or safety of patients;

children.label: III
children.text: for which there are not a minimum number (as determined by the Secretary) of measures that apply to the hospital for the performance period for such fiscal year; or

children.label: IV
children.text: for which there are not a minimum number (as determined by the Secretary) of cases for the measures that apply to the hospital for the performance period for such fiscal year.

children.heading: Independent analysis
children.label: iii Independent analysis
children.text: For purposes of determining the minimum numbers under subclauses (III) and (IV) of clause (ii), the Secretary shall have conducted an independent analysis of what numbers are appropriate.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: iv Exemption
children.text: In the case of a hospital that is paid under , the Secretary may exempt such hospital from the application of this subsection if the State which is paid under such section submits an annual report to the Secretary describing how a similar program in the State for a participating hospital or hospitals achieves or surpasses the measured results in terms of patient health outcomes and cost savings established under this subsection.section 1395f(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall select measures, other than measures of readmissions, for purposes of the Program. Such measures shall be selected from the measures specified under subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii).

children.label: aa
children.text: Acute myocardial infarction (AMI).

children.label: bb
children.text: Heart failure.

children.label: cc
children.text: Pneumonia.

children.label: dd
children.text: Surgeries, as measured by the Surgical Care Improvement Project (formerly referred to as “Surgical Infection Prevention” for discharges occurring before July 2006).

children.label: ee
children.text: Healthcare-associated infections, as measured by the prevention metrics and targets established in the HHS Action Plan to Prevent Healthcare-Associated Infections (or any successor plan) of the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: HCAHPS
children.label: II HCAHPS
children.text: Measures selected under subparagraph (A) shall be related to the Hospital Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems survey (HCAHPS).

children.heading: Inclusion of efficiency measures
children.label: ii Inclusion of efficiency measures
children.text: For value-based incentive payments made with respect to discharges occurring during fiscal year 2014 or a subsequent fiscal year, the Secretary shall ensure that measures selected under subparagraph (A) include efficiency measures, including measures of “Medicare spending per beneficiary”. Such measures shall be adjusted for factors such as age, sex, race, severity of illness, and other factors that the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: HCAHPS pain questions
children.label: iii HCAHPS pain questions
children.text: The Secretary may not include under subparagraph (A) a measure that is based on the questions appearing on the Hospital Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems survey in 2018 or 2019 about communication by hospital staff with an individual about the individual’s pain.

children.heading: Time requirement for prior reporting and notice
children.label: i Time requirement for prior reporting and notice
children.text: The Secretary may not select a measure under subparagraph (A) for use under the Program with respect to a performance period for a fiscal year (as established under paragraph (4)) unless such measure has been specified under subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii) and included on the Hospital Compare Internet website for at least 1 year prior to the beginning of such performance period.

children.heading: Measure not applicable unless hospital furnishes services appropriate to the measure
children.label: ii Measure not applicable unless hospital furnishes services appropriate to the measure
children.text: A measure selected under subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a hospital if such hospital does not furnish services appropriate to such measure.

children.heading: Replacing measures
children.label: D Replacing measures
children.text: Subclause (VI) of subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii) shall apply to measures selected under subparagraph (A) in the same manner as such subclause applies to measures selected under such subsection.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish performance standards with respect to measures selected under paragraph (2) for a performance period for a fiscal year (as established under paragraph (4)).

children.heading: Achievement and improvement
children.label: B Achievement and improvement
children.text: The performance standards established under subparagraph (A) shall include levels of achievement and improvement.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: C Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and announce the performance standards under subparagraph (A) not later than 60 days prior to the beginning of the performance period for the fiscal year involved.

children.label: i
children.text: practical experience with the measures involved, including whether a significant proportion of hospitals failed to meet the performance standard during previous performance periods;

children.label: ii
children.text: historical performance standards;

children.label: iii
children.text: improvement rates; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the opportunity for continued improvement.

children.heading: Performance period
children.label: 4 Performance period
children.text: For purposes of the Program, the Secretary shall establish the performance period for a fiscal year. Such performance period shall begin and end prior to the beginning of such fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall develop a methodology for assessing the total performance of each hospital based on performance standards with respect to the measures selected under paragraph (2) for a performance period (as established under paragraph (4)). Using such methodology, the Secretary shall provide for an assessment (in this subsection referred to as the “hospital performance score”) for each hospital for each performance period.

children.heading: Appropriate distribution
children.label: i Appropriate distribution
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the application of the methodology developed under subparagraph (A) results in an appropriate distribution of value-based incentive payments under paragraph (6) among hospitals achieving different levels of hospital performance scores, with hospitals achieving the highest hospital performance scores receiving the largest value-based incentive payments.

children.heading: Higher of achievement or improvement
children.label: ii Higher of achievement or improvement
children.text: The methodology developed under subparagraph (A) shall provide that the hospital performance score is determined using the higher of its achievement or improvement score for each measure.

children.heading: Weights
children.label: iii Weights
children.text: The methodology developed under subparagraph (A) shall provide for the assignment of weights for categories of measures as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: No minimum performance standard
children.label: iv No minimum performance standard
children.text: The Secretary shall not set a minimum performance standard in determining the hospital performance score for any hospital.

children.heading: Reflection of measures applicable to the hospital
children.label: v Reflection of measures applicable to the hospital
children.text: The hospital performance score for a hospital shall reflect the measures that apply to the hospital.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a hospital that the Secretary determines meets (or exceeds) the performance standards under paragraph (3) for the performance period for a fiscal year (as established under paragraph (4)), the Secretary shall increase the base operating DRG payment amount (as defined in paragraph (7)(D)), as determined after application of paragraph (7)(B)(i), for a hospital for each discharge occurring in such fiscal year by the value-based incentive payment amount.

children.label: i
children.text: the base operating DRG payment amount (as defined in paragraph (7)(D)) for the discharge for the hospital for such fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the value-based incentive payment percentage specified under subparagraph (C) for the hospital for such fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall specify a value-based incentive payment percentage for a hospital for a fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: such percentage is based on the hospital performance score of the hospital under paragraph (5); and

children.label: II
children.text: the total amount of value-based incentive payments under this paragraph to all hospitals in such fiscal year is equal to the total amount available for value-based incentive payments for such fiscal year under paragraph (7)(A), as estimated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: A Amount
children.text: The total amount available for value-based incentive payments under paragraph (6) for all hospitals for a fiscal year shall be equal to the total amount of reduced payments for all hospitals under subparagraph (B) for such fiscal year, as estimated by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reduce the base operating DRG payment amount (as defined in subparagraph (D)) for a hospital for each discharge in a fiscal year (beginning with fiscal year 2013) by an amount equal to the applicable percent (as defined in subparagraph (C)) of the base operating DRG payment amount for the discharge for the hospital for such fiscal year. The Secretary shall make such reductions for all hospitals in the fiscal year involved, regardless of whether or not the hospital has been determined by the Secretary to have earned a value-based incentive payment under paragraph (6) for such fiscal year.

children.heading: No effect on other payments
children.label: ii No effect on other payments
children.text: Payments described in items (aa) and (bb) of subparagraph (D)(i)(II) for a hospital shall be determined as if this subsection had not been enacted.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to fiscal year 2013, 1.0 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to fiscal year 2014, 1.25 percent;

children.label: iii
children.text: with respect to fiscal year 2015, 1.5 percent;

children.label: iv
children.text: with respect to fiscal year 2016, 1.75 percent; and

children.label: v
children.text: with respect to fiscal year 2017 and succeeding fiscal years, 2 percent.

children.heading: Base operating DRG payment amount defined
children.label: D Base operating DRG payment amount defined
I
children.text: the payment amount that would otherwise be made under subsection (d) (determined without regard to subsection (q)) for a discharge if this subsection did not apply; reduced by

children.label: aa
children.text: payments under paragraphs (5)(A), (5)(B), (5)(F), and (12) of subsection (d); and

children.label: bb
children.text: such other payments under subsection (d) determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Special rules for certain hospitals
children.label: ii Special rules for certain hospitals

children.heading: Sole community hospitals and medicare-dependent, small rural hospitals
children.label: I Sole community hospitals and medicare-dependent, small rural hospitals
children.text: In the case of a medicare-dependent, small rural hospital (with respect to discharges occurring during fiscal year 2012 and 2013) or a sole community hospital, in applying subparagraph (A)(i), the payment amount that would otherwise be made under subsection (d) shall be determined without regard to subparagraphs (I) and (L) of subsection (b)(3) and subparagraphs (D) and (G) of subsection (d)(5).

children.heading: Hospitals paid under section 1395f
children.label: II Hospitals paid under section 1395f
children.text: In the case of a hospital that is paid under , the term “base operating DRG payment amount” means the payment amount under such section.section 1395f(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Announcement of net result of adjustments
children.label: 8 Announcement of net result of adjustments
children.text: Under the Program, the Secretary shall, not later than 60 days prior to the fiscal year involved, inform each hospital of the adjustments to payments to the hospital for discharges occurring in such fiscal year under paragraphs (6) and (7)(B)(i).

children.heading: No effect in subsequent fiscal years
children.label: 9 No effect in subsequent fiscal years
children.text: The value-based incentive payment under paragraph (6) and the payment reduction under paragraph (7)(B)(i) shall each apply only with respect to the fiscal year involved, and the Secretary shall not take into account such value-based incentive payment or payment reduction in making payments to a hospital under this section in a subsequent fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: the performance of the hospital with respect to each measure that applies to the hospital;

children.label: II
children.text: the performance of the hospital with respect to each condition or procedure; and

children.label: III
children.text: the hospital performance score assessing the total performance of the hospital.

children.heading: Opportunity to review and submit corrections
children.label: ii Opportunity to review and submit corrections
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that a hospital has the opportunity to review, and submit corrections for, the information to be made public with respect to the hospital under clause (i) prior to such information being made public.

children.heading: Website
children.label: iii Website
children.text: Such information shall be posted on the Hospital Compare Internet website in an easily understandable format.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of hospitals receiving value-based incentive payments under paragraph (6) and the range and total amount of such value-based incentive payments; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of hospitals receiving less than the maximum value-based incentive payment available to the hospital for the fiscal year involved and the range and amount of such payments.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: A Appeals
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process by which hospitals may appeal the calculation of a hospital’s performance assessment with respect to the performance standards established under paragraph (3)(A) and the hospital performance score under paragraph (5). The Secretary shall ensure that such process provides for resolution of such appeals in a timely manner.

children.label: i
children.text: The methodology used to determine the amount of the value-based incentive payment under paragraph (6) and the determination of such amount.

children.label: ii
children.text: The determination of the amount of funding available for such value-based incentive payments under paragraph (7)(A) and the payment reduction under paragraph (7)(B)(i).

children.label: iii
children.text: The establishment of the performance standards under paragraph (3) and the performance period under paragraph (4).

children.label: iv
children.text: The measures specified under subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii) and the measures selected under paragraph (2).

children.label: v
children.text: The methodology developed under paragraph (5) that is used to calculate hospital performance scores and the calculation of such scores.

children.label: vi
children.text: The validation methodology specified in subsection (b)(3)(B)(viii)(XI).

children.heading: Consultation with small hospitals
children.label: C Consultation with small hospitals
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with small rural and urban hospitals on the application of the Program to such hospitals.

children.heading: Promulgation of regulations
children.label: 12 Promulgation of regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate regulations to carry out the Program, including the selection of measures under paragraph (2), the methodology developed under paragraph (5) that is used to calculate hospital performance scores, and the methodology used to determine the amount of value-based incentive payments under paragraph (6).

children.heading: Adjustment to hospital payments for hospital acquired conditions
children.label: p Adjustment to hospital payments for hospital acquired conditions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In order to provide an incentive for applicable hospitals to reduce hospital acquired conditions under this subchapter, with respect to discharges from an applicable hospital occurring during fiscal year 2015 or a subsequent fiscal year, the amount of payment under this section or , as applicable, for such discharges during the fiscal year shall be equal to 99 percent of the amount of payment that would otherwise apply to such discharges under this section or (determined after the application of subsections () and (q) and section 1395f()(4) of this title but without regard to this subsection).section 1395f(b)(3) of this titlesection 1395f(b)(3) of this titleol

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “applicable hospital” means a subsection (d) hospital that meets the criteria described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The criteria described in this subparagraph, with respect to a subsection (d) hospital, is that the subsection (d) hospital is in the top quartile of all subsection (d) hospitals, relative to the national average, of hospital acquired conditions during the applicable period, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Risk adjustment
children.label: ii Risk adjustment
children.text: In carrying out clause (i), the Secretary shall establish and apply an appropriate risk adjustment methodology.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: C Exemption
children.text: In the case of a hospital that is paid under , the Secretary may exempt such hospital from the application of this subsection if the State which is paid under such section submits an annual report to the Secretary describing how a similar program in the State for a participating hospital or hospitals achieves or surpasses the measured results in terms of patient health outcomes and cost savings established under this subsection.section 1395f(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Hospital acquired conditions
children.label: 3 Hospital acquired conditions
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “hospital acquired condition” means a condition identified for purposes of subsection (d)(4)(D)(iv) and any other condition determined appropriate by the Secretary that an individual acquires during a stay in an applicable hospital, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Applicable period
children.label: 4 Applicable period
children.text: In this subsection, the term “applicable period” means, with respect to a fiscal year, a period specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Reporting to hospitals
children.label: 5 Reporting to hospitals
children.text: Prior to fiscal year 2015 and each subsequent fiscal year, the Secretary shall provide confidential reports to applicable hospitals with respect to hospital acquired conditions of the applicable hospital during the applicable period.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make information available to the public regarding hospital acquired conditions of each applicable hospital.

children.heading: Opportunity to review and submit corrections
children.label: B Opportunity to review and submit corrections
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that an applicable hospital has the opportunity to review, and submit corrections for, the information to be made public with respect to the hospital under subparagraph (A) prior to such information being made public.

children.heading: Website
children.label: C Website
children.text: Such information shall be posted on the Hospital Compare Internet website in an easily understandable format.

children.label: A
children.text: The criteria described in paragraph (2)(A).

children.label: B
children.text: The specification of hospital acquired conditions under paragraph (3).

children.label: C
children.text: The specification of the applicable period under paragraph (4).

children.label: D
children.text: The provision of reports to applicable hospitals under paragraph (5) and the information made available to the public under paragraph (6).

children.label: A
children.text: the base operating DRG payment amount (as defined in paragraph (2)) for the discharge; and

children.label: B
children.text: the adjustment factor (described in paragraph (3)(A)) for the hospital for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Base operating DRG payment amount defined
children.label: 2 Base operating DRG payment amount defined
i
children.text: the payment amount that would otherwise be made under subsection (d) (determined without regard to subsection ()) for a discharge if this subsection did not apply; reduced byo

children.label: ii
children.text: any portion of such payment amount that is attributable to payments under paragraphs (5)(A), (5)(B), (5)(F), and (12) of subsection (d).

children.heading: Special rules for certain hospitals
children.label: B Special rules for certain hospitals

children.heading: Sole community hospitals and medicare-dependent, small rural hospitals
children.label: i Sole community hospitals and medicare-dependent, small rural hospitals
children.text: In the case of a medicare-dependent, small rural hospital (with respect to discharges occurring during fiscal years 2012 and 2013) or a sole community hospital, in applying subparagraph (A)(i), the payment amount that would otherwise be made under subsection (d) shall be determined without regard to subparagraphs (I) and (L) of subsection (b)(3) and subparagraphs (D) and (G) of subsection (d)(5).

children.heading: Hospitals paid undersection 1395f of this title
children.label: ii Hospitals paid undersection 1395f of this title
children.text: In the case of a hospital that is paid under , the Secretary may exempt such hospitals provided that States paid under such section submit an annual report to the Secretary describing how a similar program in the State for a participating hospital or hospitals achieves or surpasses the measured results in terms of patient health outcomes and cost savings established herein with respect to this section.section 1395f(b)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the ratio described in subparagraph (B) for the hospital for the applicable period (as defined in paragraph (5)(D)) for such fiscal year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the floor adjustment factor specified in subparagraph (C).

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate payments for excess readmissions (as defined in paragraph (4)(A)) with respect to an applicable hospital for the applicable period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate payments for all discharges (as defined in paragraph (4)(B)) with respect to such applicable hospital for such applicable period.

children.label: i
children.text: fiscal year 2013 is 0.99;

children.label: ii
children.text: fiscal year 2014 is 0.98; or

children.label: iii
children.text: fiscal year 2015 and subsequent fiscal years is 0.97.

children.heading: Transitional adjustment for dual eligibles
children.label: D Transitional adjustment for dual eligibles

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In determining a hospital’s adjustment factor under this paragraph for purposes of making payments for discharges occurring during and after fiscal year 2019, and before the application of clause (i) of subparagraph (E), the Secretary shall assign hospitals to groups (as defined by the Secretary under clause (ii)) and apply the applicable provisions of this subsection using a methodology in a manner that allows for separate comparison of hospitals within each such group, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Defining groups
children.label: ii Defining groups
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the Secretary shall define groups of hospitals, based on their overall proportion, of the inpatients who are entitled to, or enrolled for, benefits under part A, and who are full-benefit dual eligible individuals (as defined in ). In defining groups, the Secretary shall consult the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission and may consider the analysis done by such Commission in preparing the portion of its report submitted to Congress in June 2013 relating to readmissions.section 1396u–5(c)(6) of this title

children.heading: Minimizing reporting burden on hospitals
children.label: iii Minimizing reporting burden on hospitals
children.text: In carrying out this subparagraph, the Secretary shall not impose any additional reporting requirements on hospitals.

children.heading: Budget neutral design methodology
children.label: iv Budget neutral design methodology
children.text: The Secretary shall design the methodology to implement this subparagraph so that the estimated total amount of reductions in payments under this subsection equals the estimated total amount of reductions in payments that would otherwise occur under this subsection if this subparagraph did not apply.

children.heading: Consideration of recommendations in IMPACT reports
children.label: i Consideration of recommendations in IMPACT reports
children.text: The Secretary may take into account the studies conducted and the recommendations made by the Secretary under section 2(d)(1) of the IMPACT Act of 2014 (; note) with respect to the application under this subsection of risk adjustment methodologies. Nothing in this clause shall be construed as precluding consideration of the use of groupings of hospitals.Public Law 113–18542 U.S.C. 1395lll

children.heading: Consideration of exclusion of patient cases based on V or other appropriate codes
children.label: ii Consideration of exclusion of patient cases based on V or other appropriate codes
children.text: In promulgating regulations to carry out this subsection with respect to discharges occurring after fiscal year 2018, the Secretary may consider the use of V or other ICD-related codes for removal of a readmission. The Secretary may consider modifying measures under this subsection to incorporate V or other ICD-related codes at the same time as other changes are being made under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Removal of certain readmissions
children.label: iii Removal of certain readmissions
children.text: In promulgating regulations to carry out this subsection, with respect to discharges occurring after fiscal year 2018, the Secretary may consider removal as a readmission of an admission that is classified within one or more of the following: transplants, end-stage renal disease, burns, trauma, psychosis, or substance abuse. The Secretary may consider modifying measures under this subsection to remove readmissions at the same time as other changes are being made under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Aggregate payments, excess readmission ratio defined
children.label: 4 Aggregate payments, excess readmission ratio defined

children.heading: Aggregate payments for excess readmissions
children.label: A Aggregate payments for excess readmissions
i
children.text: the base operating DRG payment amount for such hospital for such applicable period for such condition;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of admissions for such condition for such hospital for such applicable period; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the excess readmissions ratio (as defined in subparagraph (C)) for such hospital for such applicable period minus 1.

children.heading: Aggregate payments for all discharges
children.label: B Aggregate payments for all discharges
children.text: The term “aggregate payments for all discharges” means, for a hospital for an applicable period, the sum of the base operating DRG payment amounts for all discharges for all conditions from such hospital for such applicable period.

children.label: I
children.text: the risk adjusted readmissions based on actual readmissions, as determined consistent with a readmission measure methodology that has been endorsed under paragraph (5)(A)(ii)(I), for an applicable hospital for such condition with respect to such applicable period; to

children.label: II
children.text: the risk adjusted expected readmissions (as determined consistent with such a methodology) for such hospital for such condition with respect to such applicable period.

children.heading: Exclusion of certain readmissions
children.label: ii Exclusion of certain readmissions
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), with respect to a hospital, excess readmissions shall not include readmissions for an applicable condition for which there are fewer than a minimum number (as determined by the Secretary) of discharges for such applicable condition for the applicable period and such hospital.

children.label: i
children.text: readmissions (as defined in subparagraph (E)) that represent conditions or procedures that are high volume or high expenditures under this subchapter (or other criteria specified by the Secretary); and

children.label: I
children.text: have been endorsed by the entity with a contract under ; andsection 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: such endorsed measures have exclusions for readmissions that are unrelated to the prior discharge (such as a planned readmission or transfer to another applicable hospital).

children.heading: Expansion of applicable conditions
children.label: B Expansion of applicable conditions
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 2015, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, expand the applicable conditions beyond the 3 conditions for which measures have been endorsed as described in subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) as of , to the additional 4 conditions that have been identified by the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission in its report to Congress in June 2007 and to other conditions and procedures as determined appropriate by the Secretary. In expanding such applicable conditions, the Secretary shall seek the endorsement described in subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) but may apply such measures without such an endorsement in the case of a specified area or medical topic determined appropriate by the Secretary for which a feasible and practical measure has not been endorsed by the entity with a contract under as long as due consideration is given to measures that have been endorsed or adopted by a consensus organization identified by the Secretary.March 23, 2010section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Applicable hospital
children.label: C Applicable hospital
children.text: The term “applicable hospital” means a subsection (d) hospital or a hospital that is paid under , as the case may be.section 1395f(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Applicable period
children.label: D Applicable period
children.text: The term “applicable period” means, with respect to a fiscal year, such period as the Secretary shall specify.

children.heading: Readmission
children.label: E Readmission
children.text: The term “readmission” means, in the case of an individual who is discharged from an applicable hospital, the admission of the individual to the same or another applicable hospital within a time period specified by the Secretary from the date of such discharge. Insofar as the discharge relates to an applicable condition for which there is an endorsed measure described in subparagraph (A)(ii)(I), such time period (such as 30 days) shall be consistent with the time period specified for such measure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make information available to the public regarding readmission rates of each subsection (d) hospital under the program.

children.heading: Opportunity to review and submit corrections
children.label: B Opportunity to review and submit corrections
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that a subsection (d) hospital has the opportunity to review, and submit corrections for, the information to be made public with respect to the hospital under subparagraph (A) prior to such information being made public.

children.heading: Website
children.label: C Website
children.text: Such information shall be posted on the Hospital Compare Internet website in an easily understandable format.

children.label: A
children.text: The determination of base operating DRG payment amounts.

children.label: B
children.text: The methodology for determining the adjustment factor under paragraph (3), including excess readmissions ratio under paragraph (4)(C), aggregate payments for excess readmissions under paragraph (4)(A), and aggregate payments for all discharges under paragraph (4)(B), and applicable periods and applicable conditions under paragraph (5).

children.label: C
children.text: The measures of readmissions as described in paragraph (5)(A)(ii).

children.heading: Calculation of readmission
children.label: A Calculation of readmission
children.text: The Secretary shall calculate readmission rates for all patients (as defined in subparagraph (D)) for a specified hospital (as defined in subparagraph (D)(ii)) for an applicable condition (as defined in paragraph (5)(B)) and other conditions deemed appropriate by the Secretary for an applicable period (as defined in paragraph (5)(D)) in the same manner as used to calculate such readmission rates for hospitals with respect to this subchapter and posted on the CMS Hospital Compare website.

children.heading: Posting of hospital specific all patient readmission rates
children.label: B Posting of hospital specific all patient readmission rates
children.text: The Secretary shall make information on all patient readmission rates calculated under subparagraph (A) available on the CMS Hospital Compare website in a form and manner determined appropriate by the Secretary. The Secretary may also make other information determined appropriate by the Secretary available on such website.

children.heading: Hospital submission of all patient data
children.label: C Hospital submission of all patient data
i
children.text: Except as provided for in clause (ii), each specified hospital (as defined in subparagraph (D)(ii)) shall submit to the Secretary, in a form, manner and time specified by the Secretary, data and information determined necessary by the Secretary for the Secretary to calculate the all patient readmission rates described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: ii
children.text: Instead of a specified hospital submitting to the Secretary the data and information described in clause (i), such data and information may be submitted to the Secretary, on behalf of such a specified hospital, by a state or an entity determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: The term “all patients” means patients who are treated on an inpatient basis and discharged from a specified hospital (as defined in clause (ii)).

children.label: ii
children.text: The term “specified hospital” means a subsection (d) hospital, hospitals described in clauses (i) through (v) of subsection (d)(1)(B) and, as determined feasible and appropriate by the Secretary, other hospitals not otherwise described in this subparagraph.

children.heading: Adjustments to medicare DSH payments
children.label: r Adjustments to medicare DSH payments
